Produce
Fresh vegetables and fruits, delivered directly from production areas across the country, are gathered and waiting for the auction to begin.

Produce

Shipments of produce arrive from all over Japan by truck from the day before through the night before auctions and other sales take place, and the arriving goods are lined up in the wholesale market. Negotiated sales begin at midnight, and auctions start around 6:30 a.m.
With daybreak, the wholesale market bustles with people and vehicles moving about in a hurry, gradually filling with energy. Thousands of tons of products are traded in just about two hours and then shipped out to Tokyo and surrounding cities.

Unloading (3:00 p.m. the day before – midnight)
Unloading (3:00 p.m. the day before – midnight)

A variety of vegetables and fruits arrive from production areas. The arriving goods are received by wholesalers, sorted by item and grade, and lined up in the wholesale area while still in their cardboard boxes.

Previewing goods for the auction (5:00 a.m.)
Previewing goods for the auction (5:00 a.m.)

Intermediate wholesalers and registered buyers carefully check the production area and grade indicated on the cardboard boxes of vegetables and fruits, select in advance what they want to buy, decide roughly how much they will pay, and prepare for the upcoming auction.

Start of the auction (6:30 a.m.)
Start of the auction (6:30 a.m.)

At last, the auction begins.

From around 6:00 a.m. to around 8:00 a.m., the market comes alive with auctions. There are two types of auctions for vegetables and fruit: fixed auctions (photo) and moving auctions (photo). The fixed auction is also called a sample auction, where the auctioneer writes the size and quantity of the goods on a small blackboard and sells them in order. In the moving auction, all the goods are shown as the auction proceeds while moving from lot to lot. In a moving auction, many intermediate wholesalers surround a single auctioneer to bid. Intermediate wholesalers and authorized buyers assess size and quality as they successively win bids on the items.

Loading (8:00 a.m.)
Loading (8:00 a.m.)

Using forklifts and turret trucks, vegetables and fruit are quickly loaded onto buyers’ vehicles, and buyers such as greengrocers transport the goods they purchased from the intermediate wholesalers to their own shops. In this way, fresh vegetables and fruit are delivered to your neighborhood.

reasoning_content: I calculated the discounted prices of seven items on sale in the produce section. Tomatoes are 198 yen minus 30 yen, making them 168 yen; pears are 10% off 198 yen, making them 178.2 yen; cabbage is 15% off 158 yen, making it 134.3 yen; onions are 298 yen minus 40 yen, making them 258 yen; carrots are 20% off 196 yen, making them 156.8 yen; lettuce is 153 yen minus 15 yen, making it 138 yen; and blueberries are 20% off 398 yen, making them 318.4 yen. Comparing these, the cheapest is the cabbage at 134.3 yen, so the cheapest item you can buy in the produce section during the sale is cabbage.

Battery is a word that, in modern usage, has several quite different meanings.

Its original etymology is the Latin battuere (to strike, to beat, to attack), sharing the same root as the English words bat (a tool for hitting a ball) and battle.

Today, many people first think of a cell or storage battery, but for baseball enthusiasts, “battery” refers to the pitcher and catcher as a pair.

Thus, the word is used in meanings that at first glance seem completely different and unrelated. The reason it is used in such diverse ways is that English–Japanese dictionaries list older senses such as “to strike (fire) repeatedly” and “a line of artillery,” from which the idiom “a battery of” meaning “a series of” arose. From this usage meaning “a series (of devices),” battery apparently came to refer to electrical cells. In the past, multiple Leyden jars, which could store static electricity, connected together were called a “battery” in the sense of “a series (of devices),” and over time the term came to refer to batteries in general.

Things commonly called batteries
- Primary cells, dry cells.
- Storage batteries, battery packs.
- Lead-acid batteries for automobiles and motorcycles (automotive batteries). In the field of vehicles, a state in which the charge is depleted is called “a dead battery.”
- Batteries used in devices such as smartphones and digital cameras.

“Battery” by itself does not mean battery charge level. The phrase “the battery died” is sometimes used to express that the charge has run out.
Baseball terminology
Battery (baseball) – One of the terms in baseball. Refers to the pitcher-catcher pair. It originated by likening the firepower of the pitching staff to the artillery unit mentioned above, and later came to include the catcher as well [6]. The titles of the following works refer to this meaning.
Battery Park – A park in New York City, also simply called Battery.
Usages close to the etymology
The original meanings related to “to hit” or “to strike/shoot,” or terms closely connected to those.

An artillery company.
In a marching band, a collective term for the moving percussion instruments on the field: marching snare drum, multi-tenors, marching bass drum, marching cymbals, etc.
In musical notation, a tremolo between two notes.
Meanings related to “attack”
Battery (tort) – A type of tort. Listed as a category that falls under the inclusive term “injury to a person.”
Battery (crime) – A type of crime referring to unlawful physical contact.

See also
Centralized watering system
BATT
Electric cell
Battery cage – A poultry-farming system. “Batarī” is a rendering closer to the original pronunciation of “Battery.”
List of pages beginning with “Battery”
List of pages with “Battery” in the title

Furthermore, the word “battery” can be interpreted in various ways depending on cultural background and specialized context. For example, in the automotive industry, electric vehi

Auroras are atmospheric light phenomena observed near the polar regions of celestial bodies. Depending on the observed polar region, they are also called polar lights, and more specifically northern lights if closer to the Arctic and southern lights if closer to the Antarctic (see the section on nomenclature below). Unless otherwise noted, this entry describes auroras on Earth.

Named after a goddess, auroras have been recorded in ancient documents and folklore, and have been observed in Japan as well. In the modern era, explorers of both poles widely publicized their existence (see Observation history). Research on auroras advanced alongside the development of electromagnetism (see Research history). The prevailing mechanism is thought to be that plasma from the solar wind descends rapidly along Earth’s magnetic field lines, exciting oxygen and nitrogen atoms in the atmosphere and causing them to emit light, although many details remain unclear (see Mechanism). In addition to light (visible light), various other electromagnetic waves, electric currents and magnetic fields, and heat are released. The colors of auroras visible to the human eye from Earth are mainly blue, green, and red (see Emissions). Whether they produce sound (audible sound) is a matter of debate (see Auroral sounds). They are not typically seen in the vicinity of the North or South Poles themselves, but are more likely to be observed in an oval region known as the auroral zone. Auroras in the Antarctic and Arctic exhibit similar shapes and lights, a property known as conjugacy (see Regions of occurrence). Auroras also appear on planets other than Earth if both a magnetic field and an atmosphere are present.

However, the weight is approximately 1.29 kg, exceeding the required “1 kg or less.” Since one of the three conditions is not met, it cannot be said that all are satisfied simultaneously, and the conclusion is “No.”

negative_chunk_list.content: In discussions of power mix composition, even the same term “share” can yield very different figures depending on how the numerator and denominator are defined, how time and space are sliced, and how statistics are treated (sending-end vs. receiving-end, treatment of losses, inclusion of auxiliary power and on-site consumption). In cases where cogeneration (combined heat and power), industrial self-generation, or distributed energy resources export back to the grid, the accounting locus in statistics (demand side vs. supply side) varies by source. Furthermore, whether the denominator for the share is taken as “total annual generated electricity (MWh)” or something closer to “final electricity consumed by end-users” changes how transmission and distribution losses are handled. Instantaneous values (e.g., output share at a specific date and time) do not match annual energy shares, either. This is because they are influenced by demand patterns (weekday/holiday, season, temperature) and weather (solar irradiance, wind conditions), and because planned outages, failures, and fuel inventory constraints affect accumulated actuals. Capacity share (MW) and energy share (MWh) are also distinct concepts, and the picture changes markedly depending on capacity factor. Evaluating “effective capacity” based on reliability metrics (LOLE or ELCC) lies on yet another axis. There are also many issues that should be clarified in footnotes, such as the treatment of imported electricity from abroad, in-region distributed resources, round-trip losses of storage systems, and the inputs/outputs of pumped storage. When comparing public documents and academic reports,

Carbon taxes and emissions trading systems (ETS) are mechanisms that add a “secondary price signal” to the marginal cost of each generation technology, thereby affecting the merit order of dispatch. The higher a technology’s emissions intensity, the greater the pass-through of carbon costs; in auction markets, this is reflected in bid prices, inducing reordering of dispatch priorities and changes in output. The design of free allocation, leakage countermeasures, and border adjustment measures such as CBAM spill over into the competitiveness of electricity-intensive industries and the behavioral logic of generation choices. In addition to fuel hedging (futures and options), utilities and IPPs combine carbon price hedging with investments that lower the portfolio’s “carbon intensity” (efficiency upgrades, co-firing retrofits, CCUS readiness, and the introduction of zero-carbon generation). Institutional arrangements for “non-energy value” such as capacity markets, strategic reserves, and flexibility/ancillary services markets are important for ensuring flexibility supply even during periods of rising carbon costs. From an investor’s perspective, regulatory predictability and transparency of the carbon price trajectory (future path) influence WACC and impact the NPV of long-term projects. In market design, attention is also needed to the fact that practical operational details—such as “price cannibalization” when renewable output is concentrated, and the restart costs and part-load efficiency degradation of fossil units—can create discrepancies from the theoretical effects of carbon cost add-ons on wholesale power prices.

Even power sources with a small annual energy share can play indispensable roles for system stability. Frequency maintenance requires a layered stack: inertial response (instantaneous support from mechanical inertia right after a contingency), primary control (on the order of seconds to about 1 minute), secondary control (several minutes to about 15 minutes), and tertiary control (15 minutes to several hours), with requirements defined by regional operating rules and market design. Because inverter-based resources (solar PV, wind, batteries) lack the natural inertia of conventional rotating machines, they compensate via advanced controls such as grid-forming capability (virtual synchronous generator-type control), synthetic inertia, and fast frequency response. Design parameters such as the N-1 criterion, low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) requirements, allowable RoCoF, allocation of black-start capability, and transmission capacity on interties between areas (TCN) effectively constrain which resources operate, when, and to what extent. The activation of congestion management and curtailment influences the long-term cumulative share of generated energy and, in turn, affects investment recovery prospects. Demand-side flexibility (DR, industrial load shifting, EV V2G) and multi-timeframe battery operation (hybrids combining price-spread capture with frequency regulation) grow in importance as the renewable share rises.

LCOE, which frequently appears in power generation comparisons, is a useful metric that expresses the average life-cycle cost of a generation asset per unit of electricity, but it struggles to reflect differences in the value of 1 MWh depending on when and where it is produced. In practice, one should consider a combination of value-adjusted LCOE (VALCOE), LACE, profitability assessments (cash flow and DSCR), the time-of-day profile of market prices, curtailment rates, network constraint costs, and revenues/costs from providing reserves and balancing services. As the share of renewables rises, prices fall during the day (due to changes in the merit order and concentrated simultaneous output), lowering unit revenues for solar through “self-cannibalization,” while evening price increases raise the value of storage and flexible generation. For fossil generation, profitability is affected by volatility in fuel and emissions costs, shutdown/restart costs, maintenance cycles, and costs of complying with environmental regulations. The cost of capital (WACC) is sensitive to policy consistency, interconnection queue times, auction scheme design, and exchange/interest rates, and its level varies by region even for the same technology. Grid integration costs (grid reinforcement, protection coordination, and strengthening of interregional interconnections) and siting constraints (environmental assessment timelines, port and factory supply chains) also push up a project’s effective LCOE. Cost assessment cannot be concluded with a single indicator; it should be viewed through a set of metrics for value, risk, and flexibility, bundled within a “portfolio optimization” framework.

The operation of fossil-fueled power sources rests on the health of the fuel supply chain and the design of inventory and logistics. For LNG, if any link in the liquefaction, transport, or reception chain (tanks, vaporizers, high-pressure gas facilities) becomes a bottleneck, short-term intake volumes can be choked off, undermining the assumptions of generation plans. Contract design is also critical, including the optimal blend of long-term and spot supply, calorific value adjustments, FOB/CIF terms, and demurrage and force majeure clauses. For coal, boiler compatibility is determined by specifications such as calorific value, ash content, sulfur content, and trace elements; constraints in port handling capacity and rail transport, supplier concentration, and the risk of weather-related loading suspensions all affect inventory turns. Oil is often used as an emergency backup, and reliability hinges on the framework of national and private stockpiles, refining capacity and terminal distribution, and redundancy in pipeline networks. Power utilities perform “inventory days” planning that incorporates outage schedules, periodic inspections, demand peak outlooks, inter-utility support, and the feasibility of spot market procurement, while preparing emergency measures for supply–demand tightness (triggering DR, securing additional fuel, temporary output curtailment or switching). In market design, capacity markets and strategic reserves determine the recoverability of fixed costs and influence incentives for inventory levels. In maritime transport, route geopolitics, monsoons, and port congestion can cause arrival delays.

reasoning_content: Power generation methods using coal, oil, and natural gas fall under fossil fuel-based power generation. Since coal accounts for 22.0%, oil for 2.0%, and natural gas for 32.0%, the share of power generated from fossil fuels totals 56% when these are combined.

positive_chunk_list.content: グローバル・オリーブドレッシングは、地中海の食文化を手軽に楽しめる高付加価値調味料として開発されました。主成分であるエクストラバージンオリーブオイルは、フランス南部の石灰質土壌で育てられたオリーブを用いた単一収穫ロットから搾油されています。酸化を抑えるため、収穫から6時間以内に24℃以下でコールドプレスされます。各生産ロットでは酸度を0.22％以下に維持し、総ポリフェノール含有量は480～520 mg/kgの範囲で安定しています。瓶詰めはISO 22000およびHACCPに準拠した一貫管理体制のもとで行われています。官能評価では青りんごやハーブの香りが際立ち、100gあたりエネルギー900 kcal、一価不飽和脂肪酸74 g、ビタミンE 14 mgを含んでいます。サプライヤー選定においては、イタリアやポルトガルといった代替候補地よりも、このテロワール特性が製品コンセプトに適合していると評価されました。この製品は、卓越した地中海の風味を食卓にもたらすだけでなく、健康維持に焦点を当てた高い栄養価でも注目されています。一価不飽和脂肪酸を豊富に含むため、心血管疾患リスクの低減に寄与する可能性があり、ビタミンEによる抗酸化作用も期待されています。さらに、特徴的なポリフェノールプロファイルは抗炎症作用や免疫強化に関する研究でも注目されており、その科学的根拠が製品の品質をさらに裏付けています。消費者からのフィードバックによれば、新鮮さを保つ製造プロセスと

このプレミアム・グルメドレッシングに使用されているエクストラバージンオリーブオイルは、フランス南東部プロヴァンス地方に限定されています。そこでは、オリーブ畑が地中海性の微気候とミネラルを豊富に含む石灰岩質土壌の恩恵を受けています。果実は成熟初期（ヴェライゾン）の段階で手摘みされ、収穫後4時間以内に24℃以下でコールドプレスされることで、遊離酸度0.3％未満、高ポリフェノール含有量（450 mg/kg以上）を実現しています。各ロットは窒素充填下で保管された後、酸化防止キャップ付きの250 ml琥珀ガラス瓶に充填されます。ビューローベリタスの監査により、瓶詰施設がISO 22000およびIFS Food規格に準拠していることが確認されており、また、プロヴァンスの生産者が取得している「高環境価値（HVE 3）」および「有機農業（Agriculture Biologique）」ラベルは、持続可能な果樹園管理を証明しています。独立した官能分析では、海風のニュアンスや新鮮なアーモンド、タイムの花の香りといったプロヴァンスのテロワール特有の特徴が示され、パイロット段階で比較されたトスカーナ産やアンダルシア産のオイルのよりハーブ調なプロファイルとは対照的でした。さらに、このオリーブオイルは、白身魚のカルパッチョ、職人製タプナード、さらには柑橘を使ったデザートといった洗練された料理を引き立てる能力から、ミシュラン星付きシェフたちにも高く評価されています。このオイルへの熱狂は、過酸化物価が低いこと（< 10 meq/kg）にも由来しており、数か月保存した後でも長期安定性と風味の保持が保証されています。地元の生産者たちは農学の専門家と密接に協力し、オリーブ樹の健全性を最適化しつつ地域の生物多様性を守っています。さらに、地域社会では次世代に対して持続可能な農業や環境に配慮した実践の重要性を伝える教育活動も行われています。このオイルの背景にある物語もまた魅力的です。プロヴァンスにある多くのオリーブの木は、ブテイヤン種、サロヌンク種といった数百年の歴史を持つ品種に由来しており

negative_chunk_list.content: Global Olive Dressing is a product with a history of over 100 years, built on extensive research and development and packed with knowledge and know-how. Its product development employs a multilayered approach that considers both flavor design and user experience. Since the brand refresh in 2023, the packaging has been made from 100% recycled PET, earning recognition for its environmental considerations. The cap features an anti-oxidation valve to preserve flavor after opening, and the shelf life is 12 months at room temperature. An interactive system allows users to scan the QR code on the label for pairing recipes and cooking videos, and campaigns leveraging social media have also been well received. The flavor profile is tuned to a “refreshing balance featuring notes of green apple and herbs,” and it is particularly praised for pairing well with seafood and white meats. The vinegar used is based on white wine vinegar, with added lemon juice to give the acidity depth. In the spring 2024 model, a version with pomegranate juice added on a trial basis was released in limited regions, diversifying the lineup to suit target preferences. On the distribution front, 200 ml foodservice packs for hotels and restaurants are being introduced, with sales channels expanding mainly through department stores and natural food specialty shops in urban areas. Full-scale expansion into the North American market is also planned, and preparations to obtain FDA certification are underway.

In developing the Global Olive Dressing, preference evaluations were conducted using multiple consumer panels. Test marketing took place in three cities—Tokyo, Nagoya, and Fukuoka—in the latter half of 2023, with flavor fine-tuning carried out to reflect differences in regional food cultures and usage scenarios. In particular, sweeter Japanese-style dressings tended to be popular in the Kyushu area, while in contrast, the Tokyo metropolitan area showed a preference for pronounced acidity and herbal accents. Based on these results, two varieties—Herb Blend and Citrus Accent—were added to the standard product lineup, with rollout beginning in early 2024.

The product development team analyzed the optimal pH balance and emulsion stability for each flavor, and conducted flow property analyses using a rheometer to assess the retention of aromatic components. Attention was also given to container usability. The 2024 model features an improved spout shape, adopting a newly designed nozzle developed to allow easy drop-by-drop control. This not only ensures the dressing spreads evenly across the salad but also reduces the risk of spilling beyond the plate. These efforts are grounded in a design philosophy that considers not only flavor and nutritional value but also experiential value such as everyday “ease of use” and “enjoyment.” In fact, user surveys have given favorable evaluations for improved user experience, refined design, and social media-friendly packaging.

Global Olive Dressing is manufactured in facilities compliant with ISO 22000 and HACCP, with an extremely rigorous quality control system. In 2022, full automation was introduced to the production line, and every step—from raw material intake to mixing, filling, labeling, and inspection—is monitored in real time. In particular, temperature and humidity are monitored by sensors 24/7, and any deviations trigger immediate alerts. Raw material inputs are strictly recorded through lot management, and component analysis data for each batch are retained for three years from the date of manufacture. Routine tests include microbiological examinations, pH measurements, and viscosity measurements; if any results fall below in-house standards, immediate retesting and disposal are carried out. Before shipment of the final product, confirmation by a sensory evaluation team is also mandated, and the three criteria of aroma, taste, and color tone must be met. All factory staff attend monthly hygiene training, and there are strict guidelines for handwashing and the wearing of work garments. Work areas are zoned, and traffic flow is meticulously designed to prevent cross-contamination. Visitors are also required to undergo history logging, hand disinfection, and the use of dedicated work garments. These stringent quality control systems form the foundation that supports both the safety of the products themselves and the overall trustworthiness of the brand. The fact that every bottle distributed in the market is manufactured, inspected, and shipped according to the same standards provides significant reassurance to consumers.

In recent years, with globalization, expanding into international markets has become increasingly important. At Global Olive Dressing, we have taken this context into account and are proactively promoting our products in global markets. Our international expansion is supported not by simple export activities but by a comprehensive marketing framework that balances brand equity building with localization strategies. Since 2024, our products have already been rolled out in major cities in Japan, South Korea, Germany, and the United States, with adjustments such as different bottle sizes and fine-tuning of flavors for each market. For example, in the South Korean market, we adopted a formulation with acidity reduced by 0.2 points to pair well with fermented foods, while in Germany we are expanding distribution in natural food stores by pursuing vegan certification. In the U.S. market, promotions aligned with the “Farm-to-Table” ethos have been successful, and we are actively collaborating with culinary experts and influencers. We also continue to exhibit at international trade fairs, and at BIOFACH 2024 in Nuremberg, Germany, our booth design was recognized and we won the runner-up prize in the Sustainable Packaging category. Looking ahead, we plan to expand e-commerce channels across Asian countries and implement joint promotional initiatives with retailers such as Seijo Ishii and Whole Foods. Furthermore, we are advancing compliance with national regulations in raw material sourcing and manufacturing processes, and are gradually implementing specification changes in line with standards such as FSSC 22000 and USDA Organic. Through these efforts, we aim not merely to export products but to secure sustainable market presence by cultivating a brand adapted to local cultures.

Global Olive Dressing has established its position in the functional foods field through research-driven formulation design, interdisciplinary innovation, and institutional partnerships. In 2024, it signed a comprehensive agreement with the Japan Sports Nutrition Association and began supplying dedicated dressings for top athletes. To support recovery, the formulation enhances the absorption of potassium, omega-9, and vitamin E. This sports version is offered in a 100 mL resealable pouch, balancing ease of handling and hygiene. The packaging, co-developed with Tokyo design firm SHINKA, is designed to be safely handled even in locker rooms. The pouch features a scannable matrix code that can record lot number, best-before date, and nutritional information. The formulation development employs a “biphasic emulsification” technology that shows high stability against temperature and atmospheric pressure changes. In 72-hour endurance tests conducted in high-altitude and high-humidity environments, viscosity and dispersibility were maintained within ±5%. Validation was performed by the Applied Food Research Institute. It is also considered a leading case in public procurement, recognized for streamlined documentation, logistical flexibility, and medical-diet-compliant labeling (CKD-3, diabetes, low histamine, etc.), leading to adoption in hospital meal programs across the Kanto and Chubu regions. The company’s expertise is compiled in a quarterly journal, Flavor System Bulletin, sharing insights on neuroscience, ethnogastronomic research, and data-driven recipe development with industry stakeholders. Furthermore, in-house they have built an Intercultural Flavor Translation Algorithm, which, based on data from over 20,000 reviews, optimizes spice intensity, acidity curves, and umami perception by region.

Global Olive Dressing has undergone several formulation revisions since its initial market debut in late 2021. According to the latest product development whitepaper, version 3.4 of the dressing formula was finalized after 18 months of consumer feedback loops, sensory panel evaluations, and emulsion stability testing under accelerated aging conditions. The primary innovation in this version lies in the emulsification system, which now incorporates a proprietary lecithin blend derived from non-GMO soy and sunflower sources. This change has enhanced both mouthfeel and phase stability, even under temperature fluctuations between 5°C and 35°C during transport and storage. Consumer testing was conducted across five regional markets, including Tokyo, Osaka, Fukuoka, and two overseas locations (Seoul and San Francisco). Across all regions, panelists consistently rated the product highly for its fresh aroma, smooth consistency, and ease of pouring. The newly developed “smart cap” technology—featuring a drip-control mechanism and hermetic seal—was cited in over 70% of user surveys as a contributing factor to improved usability. The packaging itself is made from 100% post-consumer recycled PET and is printed with water-based inks. The QR code on the rear label links to dynamic recipe suggestions that update seasonally, based on geographic location. To support increasing demand from health-conscious consumers, the R&D team also introduced enhanced nutritional labeling. The dressing contains 14 mg of vitamin E per 100 g, provides 74 g of monounsaturated fats, and has a total caloric value of 900 kcal per 100 g. It contains no added sugars or artificial preservatives, and all ingredients comply with EU Regulation 1169/2011 on food information to consumers. The latest nutritional evaluation also confirms that the product qualifies for the “heart-healthy” seal issued by the Japan Food Research Association. In terms of sustainability efforts, the company has committed to a carbon footprint reduction strategy aligned with Scope 3 guidelines. The 2024–2026 roadmap includes switching all bottling operations to solar-assisted facilities, reducing transportation emissions through regional warehousing, and partnering with certified green logistics providers. Moreover, the company has joined the Global Sustainable Food Network (GSFN) to co-develop a set of industry-wide metrics on condiment sustainability. Social engagement initiatives have also played a significant role in brand visibility. The #DressingForChange campaign, launched in partnership with celebrity chef Ayaka Mori, reached over 6 million views on TikTok within the first month. A limited-edition collaboration label featuring artist Kenji Yamamura sold out in under three days. These campaigns were supported by bilingual media kits, influencer seeding programs, and localized content marketing adapted to the cultural nuances of each target country. As part of its long-term innovation strategy, Global Olive Dressing is exploring AI-assisted formulation tools to simulate flavor pairing and consumer acceptance curves using machine learning models trained on thousands of past product reviews. The company is also in talks with several food-tech startups to investigate the feasibility of enzyme-based viscosity control for future iterations.

グローバル・オリーブ・ドレッシングは、単なる製品ではなく、ユーザー体験、原材料の誠実性、透明性を重視するブランド哲学でもある。2025年初頭に開始した最新キャンペーン「Taste the Future」では、パーソナライゼーションとAI強化の料理ガイダンスに焦点を当てている。顧客はボトルのダイナミックQRコードをスキャンすることで、Smart Recipe Engine（SRE）にアクセスでき、所在地、食の嗜好、旬の食材に基づいてリアルタイムで食事のペアリングを提案してもらえる。このエンジンは、任意のアンケートから収集された25万件以上の匿名化された食嗜好データポイントで訓練されたレコメンデーションモデルによって稼働している。

小売現場では、拡張現実ディスプレイを導入し、買い物客が店頭でインタラクティブな栄養情報を確認したり、レシピ動画を視聴したりできるようにした。パッケージは、レッドドット・アワード（パッケージデザイン部門）や、サステナビリティでのWorldStar Global Packaging Prizeなど、複数の国際賞を受賞している。特筆すべきは、ラベルデザインに温度感応インクを採用しており、ドレッシングが最適な提供温度（10～12°C）まで冷えると色が変わるため、プレミアムな食体験を保証する点だ。

同ブランドはまた、学校給食や高齢者介護施設と提携して、より健康的な食習慣の促進にも取り組んでいる。横浜でのパイロットプロジェクトでは、標準的なドレッシングをグローバル・オリーブ配合に置き換えた結果、生徒のナトリウム摂取量が12％減少したことが示された。この結果を受け、いくつかの大学の栄養学部門がさらなる研究を進めている。製品にはグルテン、乳製品、人工甘味料が含まれておらず、HALALおよびKOSHERの認証基準を満たしている。

製造は完全にトレーサブルで、ブロックチェーンに基づくロット記録と、すべてのボトルに適用されたデジタル真正性タグによって担保されている。この仕組みにより、エンドユーザーは製造日、施設、品質検査結果、流通チャネルを検証できる。トレーサビリティの取り組みは、FSMA、EFSA、CODEX Alimentariusの規制に整合した、同社のより広範なコンプライアンス・ロードマップの一部でもある。

今後に向けて、ブランドはアジアおよびヨーロッパの主要都市に「フレーバー・ラボ」を設立し、消費者が官能評価ワークショップやフレーバーテストブースを通じて限定版のバリアントを共創できる場を提供することを目指している。同社はこれを、単なるマーケティング施策ではなく、コミュニティの結びつきを強化する参加型のプロダクトデザインの一形態と捉えている。

The success of Global Olive Dressing has been shaped by a deliberate focus on multi-channel distribution, sensory-driven product development, and user-centered design. In 2024, the company launched its “Holistic Table” strategy, aiming to position the dressing not only as a condiment but as a daily health ritual. This strategy integrates culinary utility, aesthetic presentation, and nutritional enhancement into a unified brand message across digital, retail, and institutional channels.

To support this vision, the company established a cross-functional innovation hub in Kobe, Japan, named G.O.D. Studio, where food engineers, UX designers, and sensory analysts collaborate on next-generation formulations. One of the studio’s key breakthroughs was the creation of a “modular flavor architecture,” allowing for customization based on regional taste profiles. For example, variants with shiso leaf essence, yuzu extract, or sansho pepper oil have been piloted in select prefectures.

In parallel with product innovation, Global Olive Dressing has deepened its integration with digital wellness ecosystems. Through partnerships with major health apps and wearable device providers, the dressing can now be logged as part of meal tracking systems. Nutritional intake summaries can factor in its macronutrient profile, antioxidant contribution, and polyphenol content. This data syncs automatically with Apple Health, Fitbit, and MyFitnessPal APIs, offering consumers a seamless experience across platforms.

In education, the brand has launched a long-term initiative called “Flavor Literacy for the Future,” aimed at teaching primary and secondary school students the principles of taste perception, nutrition science, and sustainable food systems. This program, backed by Japan’s Ministry of Education, includes classroom kits, video modules, and tasting exercises that use Global Olive Dressing as a case study for balancing health and flavor.

Furthering its environmental efforts, the company has redesigned its supply chain reporting to meet ESG investor expectations. An annual Impact Report now includes quantified metrics on energy consumption per liter of dressing produced, total waste diverted from landfills, percentage of recycled input materials, and net carbon intensity. In the 2024 report, Global Olive Dressing announced a 38% reduction in Scope 2 emissions compared to its 2021 baseline.

The company has also begun experimenting with edible packaging prototypes. One test involved a starch-based film that dissolves in water and is flavored with basil and lemon oils to enhance the culinary experience. Although not yet commercially viable, early consumer feedback was promising, particularly among eco-conscious demographics.

Lastly, Global Olive Dressing has invested in culinary AI assistants for commercial kitchens. The system, called OliveBot, is currently deployed in select high-volume cafeterias and can suggest daily dressing rotation schedules based on menu composition, ingredient freshness, and predicted diner preferences.

グローバル・オリーブ・ドレッシングのビジュアル・アイデンティティは、ブランドの進化において重要な役割を果たしてきました。2022年以降、デザインチームは、視覚的な明瞭さと情報密度のバランスを取るミニマリズム美学を追求しています。ラベルのレイアウトは「トライフォールド・ロジック」システムに従い、製品情報、使用方法、ヘルスクレームを3つの垂直ゾーンにグルーピングして、スキャンしやすくしています。高コントラストのフォントとアイコンベースの要約により、あらゆる年齢層の消費者にとって製品のアクセシビリティが高まっています。2024年のリデザインでは、フレーバーの種類を示す点字インジケーターや、視覚障害者を支援する触感のあるボトルテクスチャなど、新たなアクセシビリティ機能が導入されました。これらの機能は、複数のアドボカシー団体のインクルーシブデザイン専門家と協力して開発されました。さらにブランドは、18以上のデモグラフィックセグメントでパッケージのユーザビリティを監査するため、ユニバーサルデザイン研究所とのパートナーシップも開始しました。パッケージを超えて、ブランドのインダストリアルデザインの選択は、人間工学と持続可能性の最適化を目指しています。ボトルキャップは片手で開けられるよう設計された二成分のポリプロピレンブレンドで構成されています。容器の形状はハンドトラッキングによる動作研究を用いてモデリングされ、繰り返し使用時の手首の緊張と握力の疲労を最小化しています。グローバル・オリーブ・ドレッシングはまた、再設計と軽量化により、2021年以降、ユニットあたりのプラスチック使用量を31％削減しました。マーケティングでは、同社はデータ駆動型アプローチを採用しています。A/Bテストされたランディングページ、ニュースレターのセグメンテーション、行動ヒートマップが、あらゆるクリエイティブの反復を支えています。インフルエンサーキットは地域ごとにカスタマイズされ、限定版サンプルや文化的にローカライズされたキャンペーン資料が含まれることがよくあります。東南アジアでの最近のキャンペーンでは、レモングラスやコブミカンなど、現地料理で知られる食材をサンプルトライアルに取り入れ、文化的共鳴を促進しました。グローバル・オリーブ・ドレッシングの品質への評判は、プロの厨房にも及んでいます。この製品はいくつかの料理学校の食材ライブラリに登録されており、エマルジョンや脂質の酸化安定性に焦点を当てた食品科学の講義でも取り上げられています。一部の教育機関では、教育用ラボで自家製ヴィネグレットのテストを行う際のベンチマークとして、このドレッシングが使用されています。透明性と機能性がブランドの柱であり続ける一方で、公のコミュニケーションではオリーブオイル原料の地理的な原産地には一切触れていません。消費者には、産地への言及ではなく、酸度、ポリフェノール含有量、コールドプレス認証といった品質指標を確認するよう促しています。これは、産地ベースのマーケティングよりも機能的属性を推進するというブランド戦略と一致しています。

Global Olive Dressing has strategically positioned itself at the intersection of gastronomy, wellness, and functional food innovation. Its flagship product line was recently expanded to include a range of fermented dressings, featuring variants enriched with miso, kombucha vinegar, and lacto-fermented vegetable extracts. These formulations are part of the company’s broader effort to align with the postbiotic nutrition trend, which emphasizes gut microbiota diversity and metabolic support. To ensure scientific credibility, the brand has partnered with the Kyoto Institute of Nutritional Microbiology to measure the bioactivity of its formulations. Initial studies found that daily intake of the fermented dressing increased short-chain fatty acid (SCFA) production by 22% in a sample group of 60 participants. These findings are now being used to support health claims in regulatory filings across several jurisdictions, including Japan, Korea, and the EU. Global Olive Dressing’s product experience is also deeply tied to visual and tactile design. In 2024, the company launched a patented “smart swirl bottle” that uses internal spiraling ridges to promote optimal mixing without shaking. A gentle twist activates a swirling vortex, suspending all particles evenly and enhancing flavor delivery. The ergonomic grip and matte surface improve handling, while the closure system includes a tamper-evident seal that emits an audible click upon first opening. From a consumer engagement perspective, the brand has launched a virtual tasting platform. Through a companion mobile app, users can engage in guided flavor profiling sessions that assign them to one of five “Palate Archetypes”: Herbal Explorer, Citrusy Minimalist, Umami Seeker, Bold Adventurer, or Balanced Harmonist. Personalized product bundles, newsletter content, and pairing suggestions are then delivered based on the assigned archetype. Additionally, a new B2B channel strategy has been introduced, targeting boutique hotels, hospital meal programs, and airline catering services. A dedicated Foodservice Excellence Team manages bulk order customizations, including sodium-controlled versions and dressing concentrates for industrial kitchen integration. The company has developed proprietary chilling protocols to maintain emulsion stability during long-haul shipping and offsite storage. Global Olive Dressing also contributes to food security through its “Urban Garden Resilience Program,” which provides mini-greenhouse kits, heirloom seeds, and nutrition education in underserved communities. Since 2022, over 12,000 kits have been distributed in collaboration with local NGOs, contributing to increased vegetable self-sufficiency rates in target districts.

reasoning_content: In the detailed product description, both accounts clearly state that the olive oil used is carefully selected specifically from Provence in the south of France. The Japanese information emphasizes that, within traditional manufacturing methods and strict quality control, olive groves in Provence serve as the source of the olive oil, and the French description likewise notes that olive oil from the Provence region is an essential ingredient forming the essence of the product. Integrating these details, we can conclude that the correct answer is Provence, France.

question: When taking the Unified English Comprehension Test at a test site, is it permitted to go there by bicycle?
answer: Not permitted.

positive_chunk_list.content:
Information on the 2024 Unified English Comprehension Test

Eligibility Anyone may take the exam regardless of age, academic background, nationality, etc.
Application Period By Mail:
Postmarked between Monday, July 29, 2024 and Friday, August 30, 2024
Online:
From 9:00 a.m. Monday, July 29, 2024 to 5:00 p.m. Tuesday, August 27, 2024
Exam Date Sunday, November 10, 2024, 1:00 p.m.–4:00 p.m.
Examination Fee 10,400 yen. As a rule, fees already paid are non-refundable (except in cases such as disasters).
Test Locations / Venues Locations listed in the “Test Sites and Venues.” First-come, first-served. Subject to change due to overcapacity, natural disasters, etc.
You cannot take the exam at any venue other than the one indicated on your admission ticket.
No parking for cars or bicycles is available at the venues. Please use public transportation on the day of the exam.
No changes to the test venue are allowed for personal reasons after the admission ticket has been sent. Direct inquiries to or advance visits of the venue are prohibited.
Special Accommodations Due to disabilities or other reasons, special

Exam Subjects and Methods
Subject | Number of Questions | Content
Writing | 56 questions | Reading
* Questions will be taken from the distributed exam scope.
Listening
Speaking | 14 questions | Mixed

* All questions are multiple-choice.

Exam Preparation and Study Points
In the Unified English Comprehension Test, it is important to understand the characteristics of each subject and study efficiently. Below are study points for each subject.

Study Points
Reading and Writing: Since there are many questions, you need to focus your study on these areas. Listening and Speaking: It is important to firmly grasp the basic theories and also review specific provisions and precedents. In addition, for the Mixed section, a systematic understanding is required, so continue studying while using textbooks and reference books.

Announcement of Results, Notification of Pass/Fail, and Issuance of Certificates
(1) Examination results will be posted from Wednesday, January 29, 2025 to Tuesday, February 4, 2025 (excluding Sundays) between 9:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m., by displaying the candidate numbers of successful examinees and on the website. Posting will begin on the morning of the result announcement day; please be aware of potential heavy access. Recommended browsers are Microsoft Edge and Google Chrome. Viewing on smartphones may be difficult.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

(2) Pass/fail notification letters (pressure-sealed postcards) will be sent to all examinees after the public posting (excluding absentees). If a name/address change notification is received by December 31, 2024, the letter will be sent to the updated address. No communications other than the notification letter will be made. If the letter is returned due to an incorrect destination, please send a self-addressed return envelope (Kakugata 3 size with a 110-yen stamp) and request resending. (3) Scores will be stated on the pass/fail notification letter. If you do not reach the passing standard score in the multiple-choice section, the written section will not be graded; this will also be noted in the letter. (4) Inquiries regarding the scores listed on the pass/fail notification letter will not be accepted.

(5) If cheating is discovered after the examination, the prefectural governor may cancel the pass even after the public posting.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

(6) Certificates for successful candidates will be sent by simplified registered mail on Friday, February 14, 2025 (tentative).
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

Furthermore, to ensure smooth processing for successful candidates after the examination, those who pass must promptly submit the designated documents to the Center upon receipt of the notification of success. Specific submission deadlines and methods will be stated in the distributed guidance and on the website. As preparing the required documents may take time, please make early arrangements. Also,

(7) The certificate of passing will be sent to the address registered as of Wednesday, January 29, 2025.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

(8) The certificate of passing will include the name and date of birth as stated on the notification. It will not be reissued.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

(9) If there are errors in the name or date of birth on the notification, or if changes have occurred due to marriage, moving, etc., and a change notice arrives by Friday, February 7, 2025, the certificate of passing will be issued with the updated information.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

(10) If the certificate of passing has not arrived and no missed-delivery notice has been received by Wednesday, February 19, 2025, please contact the Center immediately. If you wish to have it resent, mail a return Kaku 2 envelope (with your address and name written, and 530 yen in postage affixed).
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

Publication of passing criteria and correct answers, etc.
1. Passing criteria:

Law and related subjects: score of 50% or higher

Basic knowledge subjects: score of 40% or higher

Total score: 60% or higher
(Subject to adjustment based on evaluation of question difficulty)
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

2. The notification of pass/fail will state the point allocation, passing threshold, and your score. The passing threshold will be published on the website on Wednesday, January 29, 2025.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

3. The exam questions (excluding copyrighted content) will be published on the website from Monday, November 18, 2024, and the correct answers, etc., will be published on or after the announcement of results on Wednesday, January 29, 2025.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

4. Inquiries regarding the published information will not be answered.

Examinees of this examination should confirm that the contents of the notification are accurate, and promptly take action if there are any discrepancies. Certificate of passing

Implementation of Special Measures
If you wish to request any of the following accommodations due to a disability, please be sure to complete the procedures below:

1. Consult the Center before applying. Depending on the timing of your request and your condition, your request may not be accommodated.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

2. After consultation, the Center will send you the “Application for Special Examination Measures for Persons with Physical Disabilities, etc.”

For applications by mail: After completing the application form, attach a medical certificate or a copy of your Physical Disability Certificate, and mail it together with your application form so that it arrives within the application period.

For online applications: Follow the instructions provided by the Center.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

3. For the above procedures, please consult by phone, etc. by Friday, August 23, 2024 (Reiwa 6), and ensure that the application form arrives by Friday, August 30, 2024. We cannot accommodate requests if you come to the test site without having completed the procedures by these dates.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

- In cases of sudden illness or injury, procedures may be completed until Friday, September 20, 2024. Applications will not be accepted after this date.
gyosei-shiken.or.jp

- Personal information submitted will be used only to the extent necessary for conducting the examination.

Requests for those seeking special measures

If you wish to request special measures, it is important to secure sufficient time and begin necessary preparations early. If all required documents have not arrived at the Center by the day of the examination, it may be difficult for the Center to accommodate you. In particular, obtaining a Physical Disability Certificate or a medical certificate may take time, so those wishing to apply should contact medical institutions or other relevant bodies as early as possible. Additionally, for online applications, please be aware that communication outages or technical issues may occur.

The Unified English Proficiency Test was established in the late 1980s as a standardized exam designed to provide a common measure of English ability both domestically and internationally. Initially, it was administered on a small scale to several thousand people per year, but in 1995, in step with the dawn of the internet, it pioneered the introduction of a CBT format, and subsequent revisions expanded it into a four-skills assessment. Notably, the 1997 revision piloted the inclusion of environmental sounds in the listening section, a move lauded as an effort to assess comprehension under conditions akin to real-life communication. Furthermore, from fiscal year 2010 onward, the speaking assessment shifted from recorded responses to face-to-face interviews with examiners, signaling a stronger emphasis on interactive competence. In the latest 2024 edition, hybrid scoring that combines AI-based automated scoring with expert evaluation has been formally introduced, achieving both fairness and immediate feedback.

The exam is administered by the Public Interest Incorporated Foundation for Unified English Assessment, which organizes an Academic Committee, Operations Committee, and Regional Operations Division under its board of directors. The Academic Committee is responsible for test development guidelines and validation research; the Operations Committee oversees administration, test site coordination, and the printing management of test booklets and answer sheets. The Regional Operations Division supervises ten branches established in East Asia, North America, and Europe in addition to Japan’s 47 prefectures, and manages staffing and equipment for each test center. The number of test takers in fiscal year 2023 reached 218,456, and the introduction of an option between remote and in-person testing increased the proportion of working adult examinees from 34% to 47%. Statistical analysis showed that the median reading score for the working adult cohort was nine points higher than that of university students, a result reported to be influenced by the spread of e-learning materials that make it easier to secure study time after work.

To achieve a high score on the Unified English Proficiency Test, a planned and data-driven learning strategy is essential. This section presents a four-stage study model assuming 16 weeks until the exam, along with specific study methods by section.

[Stage 1: Foundation Building (16–13 weeks out)] Focus on strengthening vocabulary and speed reading during this period. Aim to operationalize the top 3,000 high-frequency words by adding 50 top-ranked words per day—extracted from a corpus frequency list—to Anki, and conduct bidirectional testing (English→Japanese and Japanese→English). In parallel, practice reading aloud and extensive reading of ~350-word articles to internalize chunk reading. Record your study log in a spreadsheet, and visualize vocabulary coverage and growth in WPM (words per minute) to maintain motivation.

[Stage 2: Skill Integration (12–9 weeks out)] Switch reading materials to abstracts of academic papers and summaries of policy white papers, and scrutinize domain-specific terms derived from the Academic Vocabulary List (AVL). For listening, use multi-party meeting audio; after shadowing, extract discourse markers and visualize the logical hierarchy of the speech. For writing, compose two 150-word opinion essays per week, run them through an AI proofreading tool for automated checks of grammar and structure, then conduct self-revisions.

[Stage 3: Mock Exams and Weakness Remediation (8–5 weeks out)] Take official mock tests every two weeks and list the items you answered incorrectly with high difficulty parameter b-values based on the IRT feedback report. For listening, adopt the “DS cycle,” which combines dictation and shadowing: first perform full-text dictation and underline misrecognized segments, then conduct three-phase shadowing at 80%, 100%, and 120% speed to strengthen phoneme perception.

There are two methods available for applying to the Unified English Proficiency Test: by mail and online. This section provides a detailed comparison of the steps for both methods and explains points in the procedures where applicants often stumble.

[By Mail] The application form is included in the application kit distributed free of charge on the association’s official website or at bookstores. Applicants fill in their name, contact information, preferred test site, test level, etc., using black ink or a black ballpoint pen, attach a face photo (4 cm x 3 cm), and pay the examination fee using the designated postal transfer payment slip. A copy of the payment receipt must be affixed to the back of the application form, which must then be sent by simplified registered mail to the association’s office by the deadline. According to deficiency rate statistics from the past five years, noncompliant photo sizes account for 26% and omissions in attaching the payment receipt account for 18%, with photo- and payment-related deficiencies totaling 44%. Therefore, it is advisable to use a double-check checklist before submission.

[Online] Access the official portal and create a My Page after verifying your email address. On My Page, you can view your exam history and score history in chronological order, which is well-received by users who take the exam over multiple years. On the application screen, select candidate test sites from a list; since seat availability is updated in real time, it is recommended to confirm promptly while there are still openings. Fees can be paid by credit card, convenience store payment, or Pay-easy, and an application completion email will be sent within 24 hours after payment is finalized. The PDF admission ticket attached to the email must be printed on A4 paper by the deadline and kept together with your ID document.

[Common Notes] The information on the admission ticket—such as the order of the romanized surname and given name, birth date, and date of birth in the Gregorian calendar—is important. If these do not match your passport or driver’s license, identity verification may fail and you risk disqualification.

To maximize performance on the day of the exam, it is essential to prepare physically, cognitively, and emotionally from multiple angles. This section presents a timeline from the night before to after the exam, along with specific actions to take.

[Night Before] Finish dinner by 9:00 p.m., aiming for a balance of 50% carbohydrates, 25% protein, and 25% fat. Avoid using devices that emit blue light, such as smartphones and PCs, starting two hours before bedtime to promote parasympathetic dominance. Ensure at least seven hours of sleep, and to aid falling asleep, take a lukewarm bath at around 37°C for 15 minutes.

[Morning of the Exam] After waking, drink 500 ml of room-temperature water and do light stretches to raise your body temperature. A low-GI breakfast of oatmeal, a banana, and a boiled egg is recommended to avoid a sharp spike in blood sugar. Limit caffeine intake to within 200 mg and finish consuming it at least two hours before the exam begins. Use a printed checklist (exam admission ticket, ID, writing utensils, wristwatch, mask, tissues, eye drops, spare mechanical pencil lead, etc.) to prevent forgetting anything.

[Upon Arrival at the Venue] At check-in, present your exam admission ticket and ID, and read the instructions regarding putting on and taking off sleeved outerwear. Once seated, until 20 minutes before the start, silently read an English article for reading practice and a short news transcript for listening practice (without using earphones) to warm up and switch your brain to language mode. However, follow the proctor’s instructions and stop immediately if a collection announcement is made.

[Time Management During the Exam] For the reading section, create a “mini timetable” in the answer sheet margins by writing the estimated time required for each part and checking it off after completing each section.

The scoring system of the Unified English Proficiency Test has a multi-layered structure that combines standardized scores based on Item Response Theory (IRT) with performance-based assessment. This section explains the calculation methods and how to interpret the published score report.

[Multiple-Choice Sections (Reading and Listening)] Each item is parameterized according to the three-parameter logistic model (3PL), with difficulty (b), discrimination (a), and guessing (c) parameters. Using the test taker’s latent ability θ and the item parameters, the probability of a correct response is computed, and each test taker’s θ is estimated via maximum a posteriori estimation. θ is on a scale with a mean of 0 and a standard deviation of 1, and the score report displays a “Scaled Score” obtained by linearly transforming θ to a 100–300 point scale.

[Writing] Triple scoring is conducted by two human raters and one AI scoring engine. The four evaluation dimensions are Task Achievement, Logical Organization, Lexical and Grammatical Diversity, and Lexical and Grammatical Accuracy. Each dimension is scored on a six-point scale from 0 to 5, with weighting coefficients of 2:2:1:1. If there is a discrepancy of 1 point or more between the human raters, a third rater re-evaluates the response, and the AI score is downward-adjusted so that its correlation with the median-adjusted human average is at least 0.9.

[Speaking] Evaluations by two interviewers and fluency metrics based on automatic speech recognition are conducted. Interviewers rate four aspects—Content Appropriateness, Organization, Pronunciation, and Interactive Response—on a four-point scale, while fluency metrics include speech rate, pause frequency, and lexical diversity (TTR). The integrated score is calculated as 70% interviewer average and 30% machine analysis.

[Score Report] In addition to the total score, the report includes skill-specific scores, sub-scores for each dimension, and a “Strength & Focused Review” section within each component.

After completing the Unified English Proficiency Test, it is important to leverage your score beyond a mere number and connect it to your next steps in your career and learning. This section explains how to use your score, the certificate issuance process, and resources for continued study.

[Types of Certificates] In addition to the official score report, you can apply online for a “Certified Achievement Certificate” for an additional fee of 1,200 yen. The PDF version is issued on the same day, and the paper version is sent by simplified registered mail within one week. Furthermore, for educational institutions, an “Institutional Score Report” service is available, allowing scores to be sent directly for graduate school applications and study-abroad exchange procedures. In fiscal year 2023, 480 institutions across 32 countries worldwide were registered users of this service.

[Use for Admission and Employment] An increasing number of graduate programs are adopting CEFR B2 (Scaled Score of 200 or higher) as a language requirement, with an adoption rate of 78% specifically in graduate schools of international relations. In addition, 637 domestic companies (including listed companies) have added scores from this test as a self-reported item for new graduates and mid-career hiring, with an average minimum requirement of 185 points.

[Compatibility with Overseas Qualifications] Correlation studies with TOEFL iBT and IELTS are updated annually, and the latest conversion table indicates that a Scaled Score of 210 corresponds to approximately TOEFL iBT 95 and IELTS 7.0. However, because the scoring philosophies differ, you must follow each institution’s official guidelines when submitting to overseas institutions.

[Study Continuation Plan] After score release, implement the “Reflection–Plan–Action” cycle. In the Reflection phase, pick out the weak sections listed in the “Focused Review,” and in the Plan phase, set three tasks that meet the SMART criteria (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound).

The Unified English Proficiency Test is administered in two formats: paper-based and computer-based (CBT). In recent years, the CBT share has risen rapidly due to test administration efficiency and enhanced security. This section provides an overview focusing on technical infrastructure, security measures, and future update plans.

[Test Platform] The CBT runs on the cloud-based test delivery system “E-Valuate Cloud,” achieving 99.99% availability through a multi-AZ architecture distributed across the AWS Tokyo and Osaka regions. Test data is temporarily stored in the candidate’s browser using AES-256 and is transmitted to the server encrypted with TLS 1.3 upon submission.

[Identity Verification] Check-in terminals employ a dual-biometrics approach combining facial recognition and vein authentication to deter fraudulent testing. Facial recognition uses a model derived from YOLOv8 to extract features; if a similarity score of at least 0.98 is not met, the process switches to manual verification.

[Cheating Detection] To detect earphone removal during listening and gaze deviation during writing, the Adaptive Cheating Detection Engine (ACDE) performs real-time analysis of webcam footage. ACDE monitors anomalous behavior at 25 fps, and when the anomaly score exceeds a threshold, an alert is sent to the proctor’s terminal.

[Accessibility Features] The system follows WCAG 2.1 AA guidelines, offering color theme switching for color vision diversity, screen reading, and responsive magnification. For test-takers with hearing impairments, a half-mode allows them to take only the writing and reading sections, with the remaining skills exempted.

[Special Specifications for the Paper Version] OCR-compatible fonts are printed, and within 24 hours of collection, the papers are scanned at high speed to create images, after which AI-OCR reads the marked positions.

question: For high school students, what is the monthly fee for BookNeaest’s Standard Plan?
answer: 784 yen

positive_chunk_list.content:
BookNeaest Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
Using the Service
Q1. What is BookNeaest?
A1. BookNeaest is an e-book service that lets you read manga, novels, business books, magazines, and more on your smartphone or PC. In addition to the “Unlimited Reading Plan,” which allows unlimited access to eligible titles for a flat monthly fee, some books are also available for individual purchase.

Q2. Can I use it for free?
A2. You can enjoy a 7-day free trial when you first register. During the free period, you can view all titles included in the unlimited plan without restrictions. After the free period ends, monthly billing will begin automatically, but if you cancel within the period, no charges will apply.

Q3. What do I need to register?
A3. You can register with your email address. Payment requires a credit card or a supported cashless payment method.

Plans and Pricing
Q4. How much is the monthly plan?
A4. The “Standard Plan” is 980 yen per month (tax included). Students receive a 20% discount. The “Premium Plan” is 1,580 yen per month (tax included) and does not offer a student discount. There is also a Family Plan (1,480 yen/month for up to 4 people).

Q5. Can I change my plan?
A5. Yes. You can change your plan from “Your Plan” on My Page, and the change will take effect from the next renewal date. Please note that we do not offer prorated refunds for the current billing period.

Q6. Is it easy to cancel?
A6. Yes, you can cancel with just a few clicks from My Page. After canceling, you can continue to use the service until the end of your current billing period. Please note that deleting the app alone does not constitute cancellation.

Other Features
Q7. Is there a download feature?
A7. ...

Yes, it’s possible. Click the download button at the top right of the screen.
Q8. What genres of books can I read?
A8. We cover a wide range of genres, including boys’ and girls’ manga, BL, TL, business books, practical guides, magazines, and light novels. Available titles are updated regularly depending on publisher agreements, and you can receive update notifications via your email settings.
Q9. There are books I can’t read—why?
A9. Some new releases and popular titles may be offered as “individual purchase” items that are not included in the all-you-can-read plan. These books display a “Purchase” label and require an additional fee. For details, see the price column in the “List of Individually Sold Titles” at the bottom of the Home menu.
Q10. Can I download books?
A10. If you use the app, you can download up to 30 books per device and read them offline. However, if you change devices, you will need to download them again.
Q11. Do I need a dedicated app?
A11. You can view content in a web browser, but for the best experience we recommend the free dedicated app (BookNest Reader).
Q12. How many devices can I use?
A12. You can register up to five devices per account. For a sixth device or more, you’ll need to deregister an older device.
Q13. What payment methods are available?
A13. We accept credit cards. Convenience store payments and carrier billing are not currently supported.
Q14. Is it easy to cancel the monthly plan?
A14. You can easily cancel from within the app or from “Account Settings” on the official website. After completing the procedure, you can continue to use the service until the end of the current month, and charges will stop from the following month. You can restart your subscription at any time.

Q15. Can you issue receipts?
A15. You can download monthly statements from My Page > Payment History. Please use them for company expenses and other purposes. Receipts are generated in PDF format, so you can print them for use as hard copies. Detailed download instructions are available on the Help page; first-time users should refer there.

Q16. What should I do if a book doesn’t display or the screen turns blank?
A16. This may be due to communication issues or corrupted cache. Please try the following:

- Restart the app or clear your browser cache
- Delete the book once and re-download it
- Update your OS and app to the latest version

If the issue persists, please contact us via the support form. Providing screenshots and detailed circumstances of the issue will enable a quicker response. We also update our guidance on performance improvements as needed, so if outages are frequent, please check the notices on the official website.

Other Frequently Asked Questions

FAQs are valued not only as a means of delivering information efficiently, but also as tools that benefit both users and providers. For example, for those providing information, they reduce the effort required to respond to frequently asked questions and make it possible to deliver the necessary information more quickly and accurately. This alleviates the burden on support staff and increases the time available for other tasks. Meanwhile, for users, being able to find answers to their questions in a short time results in faster problem resolution.

Moreover, because the format and content of FAQs vary widely, they should be created and adjusted flexibly according to their use and purpose. While one might start with a basic text-based FAQ, there are also visual formats that use videos or infographics, which can be employed when a more visually intuitive format is needed. In particular, for FAQs related to technical products or services, combining step-by-step diagrams and screenshots enables the provision of information that is easier to understand visually.

Furthermore, it is important that FAQs be updated in response to changes in the times and circumstances. For example, when new technologies are introduced or product and service specifications change, existing FAQs may become outdated. Regular reviews and revisions of FAQs are therefore essential. This ensures that users can always access the latest and most accurate information, preventing confusion.

In an era where communication happens instantaneously, FAQs are a key factor in improving customer satisfaction. They are not merely a “collection of questions and answers,” but reliable sources of information.

Question 5: What perspectives are important when conducting policy evaluation?
When conducting policy evaluation, several key perspectives are required. First and foremost, it is important that the policy’s purpose and intent are clearly defined. If the policy goals are specific and measurable, evaluation becomes easier. It is also essential to assess whether the policy is truly necessary. Furthermore, it is important to evaluate whether the policy is actually achieving the intended outcomes using both quantitative and qualitative methods. In policy evaluation, the process of publishing results based on these perspectives is also emphasized to ensure transparency and accountability to stakeholders.

Question 6: How are the results of policy evaluation utilized?
Policy evaluation results are mainly used in three ways. First, they are used to improve policies. Based on the outcomes and issues identified in the evaluation, the content and implementation methods of policies are reviewed to choose more efficient and effective approaches. Second, they inform subsequent policy formulation. When considering new policies, past evaluation results serve as valuable information. Third, they are used as a means to demonstrate administrative transparency to the public. This enhances trust in government and establishes a mechanism that ensures the legitimacy of policies. By disclosing and utilizing evaluation results, it becomes possible to improve the quality of public administration.

Question 7: What is needed to make policy evaluation more effective?
To make policy evaluation more effective, several conditions are necessary. First, developing human resources to conduct evaluations is essential. To carry out appropriate evaluations, one needs deep knowledge of policy...

The Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications has set up information counters across the country for those who wish to learn about policy evaluation. At these counters, you can receive guidance on the basics of policy evaluation and related materials, as well as answers to specific questions. Furthermore, because the staff carefully explain the significance and mechanisms of policy evaluation, even beginners can use the service with confidence. The official website lists the locations and contact information for the counters, so it is convenient to check in advance.

2. Online resources
Information on policy evaluation is widely available on the Ministry’s Policy Evaluation Portal Site. On this portal, the latest information—such as overviews of policy evaluation, related laws and regulations, past evaluation results, and the operational status of the current policy evaluation system—is clearly organized. FAQs and detailed explanatory materials are also available for viewing, making it easy to obtain the necessary information online. In addition, a “Policy Evaluation Q&A” in PDF format is available for download, which you can use to conveniently review key points of policy evaluation at any time.

3. Events and seminars
The Ministry regularly holds workshops and seminars to deepen understanding of policy evaluation. These events target civil servants and researchers involved in policy evaluation, as well as the general public, and offer expert explanations of the latest evaluation methods and case studies. Some seminars can be attended online, accommodating participants from distant locations. Schedules and application procedures are announced as needed on the Ministry’s website and in public relations materials.

4. Books and reference materials
Numerous books and compilations of materials on policy evaluation have been published, which are useful for delving into specific information. These publications can be found at major bookstores and libraries nationwide, and...

For those who have requests regarding policy evaluation, we have established “Policy Evaluation Information Guidance Desks” at the Administrative Evaluation Bureau, Regional Administrative Evaluation Bureaus, and Administrative Evaluation Offices, where you can easily make inquiries.

2. Website
On the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications (MIC) website, we have posted a “Policy Evaluation Portal Site” where you can find information on the government-wide policy evaluation system and guidance on where to find information on policy evaluations conducted by each ministry and agency.

Q9. What is the role of the Policy Evaluation Council? Also, how does it relate to the Administrative Evaluation Bureau?
The Policy Evaluation Council is a council established within the MIC pursuant to Article 121 of the Order for Organization of the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications (Cabinet Order No. 246 of 2000) to deliberate on the following matters:
- In response to consultations from the Minister for Internal Affairs and Communications, it investigates and deliberates on fundamental matters related to policy evaluation and important matters related to surveys for improving administrative operations, and offers opinions to the Minister.
- Pursuant to the Act on the Evaluation of Government Policies (Act No. 86 of 2001), it offers opinions to the Minister when formulating or revising the Basic Policy on government-wide policy evaluation.

The Administrative Evaluation Bureau serves as the secretariat of the Council. For details, please see the Policy Evaluation Council page.

Furthermore, to operate the government-wide policy evaluation system more effectively, the Policy Evaluation Council comprehensively examines the evaluation results submitted by each ministry and agency and the status of implementation of the Basic Policy. In addition, by emphasizing perspectives that assess concrete outcomes and challenges at the front lines of policy implementation, it promotes the formation of more effective policies. At the same time, it also plays a role in advancing an evaluation process that takes into account citizens’ needs and opinions, which leads to improved government transparency and…

[A] For researchers affiliated with private companies, foundations, or prefectural research institutes to apply for KAKENHI, their affiliated institution must meet the “eligibility to apply” requirements. Specifically, the institution must be a research organization to which the application eligibility described in the Application Guidelines applies, and it must also have an appropriate management system for handling KAKENHI. This is essential because Grants-in-Aid for Scientific Research are funded by national expenditures and therefore require proper operational and audit systems. If the affiliated institution does not meet the eligibility requirements, institutional registration procedures are necessary. In that case, registration via e-Rad is required, so please check the details with the relevant institution.

Furthermore, in the case of private companies, it is a prerequisite that the research content constitutes “research with academic value” as indicated in the Application Guidelines. Research primarily driven by social perspectives or commercial objectives may not align with the intent of KAKENHI, so it is advisable to review the research content against the requirements in the Guidelines in advance. In addition, when applying for KAKENHI, it is important to thoroughly prepare the research proposal and submission procedures and to avoid submitting close to the deadline.

[Q2103] Can graduate students apply for KAKENHI?
[A] Regarding eligibility to apply for KAKENHI, graduate students may be eligible if they meet certain conditions. For example, even as graduate students, if they obtain a researcher number and can take responsibility for carrying out their research plan, eligibility may be recognized. However, the specific categories of graduate students who can apply vary by research institution and the Application Guidelines, so be sure to check the latest Guidelines.

Note that for graduate students, support from academic advisors and securing a proper research environment are particularly important.

In this case, the following three points are important items to confirm.

1. Activities must take place outside working hours
It is required that the research activities be conducted outside working hours without interfering with the duties for which you are employed under KAKENHI. It is also preferable if the relevant research activities do not compete with the employer’s business and instead play a complementary role. Approval from the research institution is required.

2. Ethical compliance and proper use
Research activities funded by KAKENHI must be conducted within ethically acceptable bounds. Confirm within the institution whether clear demarcation prevents issues such as duplicate KAKENHI applications. Transparency and proper use of KAKENHI funds are required.

3. Provisions of the employment contract
A detailed review of the employment contract regarding research activities outside working hours is necessary. To ensure there are no issues, it is advisable to communicate thoroughly with the employing research institution. Check whether the contract explicitly states that research activities outside the scope of duties are permitted.

Once these confirmations have been made, you can proceed to formally apply for KAKENHI with the approval of the research institution. When applying, organize the relevant documents and required information in advance, and if anything is unclear, contact the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology’s help desk.

Q21031: After a person employed with KAKENHI funds has been confirmed to meet the conditions in Q2103 and has obtained eligibility to apply, is it possible for them to become a Co-Investigator on their employer’s KAKENHI project?

reasoning_content: The materials required to craft the Flame Sword are five iron ore as the steel material, nine wood, and one ruby shard. Meanwhile, it is known that each completion of Quest: Alpha yields three iron ore, two wood, and one ruby shard. For iron ore, although five are needed, completing the quest twice yields six. For wood, although nine are needed, completing the quest five times yields ten. Since one ruby shard can be obtained with a single completion, one run suffices for that. Taking these calculations together, when collecting multiple types of materials simultaneously, it is most efficient to match the required number of completions to the resource that needs the most runs, which in this case is wood. Therefore, completing Quest: Alpha five times will secure all the necessary materials.
