[Exam Guide] Complete Guidelines for the FY2025 Qualification Exam (Primary)

1. Items to bring on the exam day
  ① Exam admission ticket (display at the top left of your desk)
  ② Graphite pencils B–2B or a 0.5 mm mechanical pencil with B lead, including spares
  ③ Plastic eraser (sand erasers not permitted)
  ④ Wristwatch without communication functions (ensure alarm is turned off)
  ⑤ Photo ID (e.g., driver’s license, employee ID, passport)
  ⑥ Up to two highlighters (orange and yellow recommended)
  ⑦ Glasses/magnifier (as needed)
  ※ Devices with communication functions, such as electronic dictionaries, smartwatches, and smart glasses, are strictly prohibited. Deposit them in a sealed bag at the venue entrance.

2. Exam day schedule
  09:30 Venue opens / Check-in begins
  09:50 Guidance and instructions
  10:00 Written exam begins
  10:30 Late entry cutoff (no entry permitted thereafter)
  12:00 Morning session ends / Exit and re-entry allowed (60-minute lunch break)
  13:00 Afternoon session begins
  15:30 Exam scheduled to end / Collection of answer sheets
  15:40 Exit complete / Q&A reception ends

3. Rules inside the exam room
  - No talking starting 30 minutes before the exam begins.
  - Put on or take off outerwear only with the proctor’s permission and do so quietly.
  - Be sure to write your name and exam number on the answer sheet in pencil.
  - Studying at your seat is allowed during the lunch break; however, only the distributed question booklet may be on the desk.
  - Beverages are limited to capped plastic bottles or screw-top cans. Paper cups and insulated bottles are not allowed.

4. Leaving and re-entering during the exam
  You may leave between 30 minutes after the start and 10 minutes before the end of each of the morning and afternoon sessions.

However, upon leaving your seat, place both the question booklet and answer sheet face down on the desk; checking smartphones and conversing are prohibited. When using the restroom, a proctor will accompany you, and any actions other than using the facilities (phone calls, conversations, consulting materials) are prohibited.

5. Handling of Cheating
   All forms of misconduct are strictly prohibited, including cheating, taking the exam by proxy, leaking exam questions or posting them on social media, recording or filming, bringing in materials obtained in advance, and sending signals to others. If misconduct is discovered, you will be required to leave immediately, disqualified from all subjects in the current year, and your eligibility to sit for the exam will be suspended for three years from the following year. Legal action may be taken depending on the severity.

6. Announcement of Results
   Approximately 45 days after the exam date, notifications will be sent to your My Page on the official website and to your registered email address. Certificates of passing will be mailed within 30 days from the announcement date. If mail is undeliverable, a re-delivery fee of 1,100 yen (tax included) will be required.

7. Contact Information
   Exam Administration Office (TEL: 03-0000-0000, weekdays 09:00–17:00) or via the inquiry form on the official website. A voice guidance line provides FAQs 24 hours a day.

────────────────────────────────────────
Supplementary Notes: The contents of these guidelines are subject to change without notice. Please check the official website regularly for the latest information. In addition, examinees must carefully read and pledge to abide by the “Examinee Guidelines” before the exam. Doing so will help prevent problems on the exam day and ensure a smooth testing environment.

[Test Room Safety, Hygiene, and Facility Use Manual] Ver. 3.2
────────────────────────────────────────
1. Air Conditioning and Ventilation
   The test room is maintained at 24±1°C and 40–60% humidity by an automatic climate control system. Individual temperature adjustments are not available. Candidates sensitive to temperature are advised to dress in layers. The ventilation system, linked to a CO₂ sensor, introduces outside air at a rate of 20 minutes per hour. Windows must remain closed.

2. Lighting and Visual Environment
   LED ceiling lights (500 lx) remain on at all times. When the proctor presents practice problems, a projector (5000 lm) displays on the front screen. Adequate illumination for writing is maintained even during projection.

3. Seating and Desks
   A fixed two-person desk measuring 900 mm wide × 600 mm deep is used as one seat. Only the admission ticket, writing instruments, test booklet, answer sheet, and a wristwatch may be placed on the desktop. Desks may not be moved. Chairs have fabric upholstery and no casters.

4. Entrances and Traffic Flow
   The test room entrance uses a double-door structure providing Dr-40 sound insulation. When leaving the room, line up in a single file and minimize door opening/closing noise. The evacuation route map is posted on the back wall.

5. ICT Equipment (Not for Candidate Use)
   Equipment includes a proctor’s PC, four surveillance cameras, a ceiling microphone audio recording system, a screen, and a Cisco router. Candidates cannot access power outlets or LAN ports from their seats.

6. Safety and Hygiene Supplies
   Alcohol-based sanitizer (70%) is installed at both the entrance and exit. Mask wearing is recommended (optional). Observe cough etiquette. Disposable gloves are available upon request.

7. Emergency Response
   In the event of an earthquake, follow the proctor’s instructions and take shelter under the desk.

[Training Program Overview] FY H35 Comprehensive Employee Career Development Training (Provisional)

■ As needed Thematic Seminars and e-Learning

■ Evaluation and Completion Criteria
- Score 80 or above on each course’s online test; pass group project presentations

[Notice] SDGs-Oriented Project Planning Course Opening Announcement (Ongoing)

1. Purpose of the Course
To provide a systematic learning opportunity for all employees, aiming to enhance the capability to plan new businesses and social contribution projects incorporating the perspective of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

2. Eligibility and Participation Conditions
All employees (from first-year hires to managers). No project experience required. No department head recommendation required; self-application. Capacity: 30 participants per session; minimum of 5 to run.

3. Schedule
Second and fourth Friday of each month, 13:00–17:00 (hybrid with online option).
First session: Friday, April 11, 2025
Theme: “SDGs Basics and Corporate Value”

4. Course Content (Examples)
- History of the SDGs and overview of the 17 goals
- Analysis of challenges in our company’s value chain (workshop)
- Social impact evaluation (Theory of Change)
- KPI/KGI setting and monitoring indicators
- Stakeholder analysis and partnership building
- Funding schemes (ESG investing and impact investing)

5. Fees and Application Method
Tuition is free. Apply via the internal LMS (Learning Management System). Registration closes once capacity is reached. If you will be absent, you must cancel at least three days before the session.

6. Completion Certification
A certificate of completion will be awarded upon attending all four sessions and passing the final deliverable presentation (5-minute pitch), and this will be reflected on the internal career portal. Graduates will be automatically registered as facilitator candidates for the following year.

[Internal Notice] Implementation Plan for the FY H35 Employee Satisfaction Survey

1. Purpose of the Survey
  To understand the actual conditions and perceptions regarding organizational culture, workplace environment, career support, benefits, and work-life balance in order to improve employee engagement.

[Conference Room B101 ICT Equipment User Manual] Ver.2025-03
────────────────────────────────────────
1. List of Installed Equipment
- 55-inch 4K display (HDMI/USB-C/wireless casting supported)
- CGTL auto-tracking camera (12x optical zoom, AI face recognition)

- Four ceiling-embedded speakers (40 W output)

2. Online Meeting Setup Procedure
1) Turn ON the main power for the ceiling microphone and camera. They are in standby when the green light is on.
2) On the touch panel, select “Online Meeting,” then tap your desired platform (selectable).
3) Connect your laptop to the desktop USB-C hub. The camera and microphone will be recognized automatically.
4) For screen sharing, use HDMI or Midheha. Shared audio is automatically mixed.
5) After finishing, tap “Shut Down Equipment” → “Reset Room” to initialize.

3. Usage Rules
- You must check “ICT Use” in the reservation system (RMS). To secure bandwidth, up to three concurrent meetings are allowed.
- When external participants are involved, always choose an encrypted meeting and send the passcode via a separate channel.
- Recording data is automatically saved to the internal Box shared folder (retention period: 90 days). Recording is prohibited for agenda items classified Confidentiality C or higher.
- Do not take the provided remote control out of the room. If lost, reimburse the actual cost of 8,000 yen.

4. Troubleshooting

[Information Systems Department Guideline 7.2] Internal Online Meeting Security Standards
────────────────────────────────────────
1. Scope
   All cloud meeting services provided company-wide and on-premises SIP endpoints. Applies regardless of meeting rooms, personal devices, or mobile devices.
2. Requirements for hosting meetings
   - Agenda with confidentiality classification B or higher → Enable waiting room (lobby) and require two-factor participant authentication (SSO + OTP).
   - External guest participation → Create a dedicated channel; shared materials expire automatically after 7 days.
   - Recordings must be automatically encrypted (AES-256) and transferred to Box in accordance with the Information Security Standards.
3. Device management
   - Company-issued PCs: Apply the latest patches and enable Host IPS. Disable autorun for USB storage.
   - BYOD: Register with MDM; jailbroken/rooted devices are not allowed.
4. Network requirements
   - Assign a dedicated QoS tag (DSCP 46) to prioritize meeting traffic bandwidth.
   - For VPN access, regularly measure speeds on UDP 3478 and TCP 443. If the median is below 3 Mbps, switch the connection.
5. Incident response
   - Upon detecting a suspicious participant → Remove immediately and automatically report to "incident@corp".
   - If a screen sharing hijack occurs → The host should "Stop all sharing" → "Lock meeting".
6. Training and audit
   - Conduct quarterly e-learning (average correct answer rate of 90% or higher) and phishing drills.
   - Store meeting logs in Splussing Cloud for 14 months; the audit department conducts an annual review.

[Extract from Gyousai University Faculty Employment Regulations] (Revised April 1, 2023)
──────────────────────────────────────────
Article 4 (Transfers and Concurrent Appointments)
Professors, associate professors, lecturers, and assistant professors may be ordered to hold concurrent appointments with other graduate schools, attached research institutes, international collaboration hubs, etc. designated by the University when necessary for education and research. In such cases, the University shall seek the opinion of the Career Development Support Committee while respecting the individual’s wishes.

Article 7 (Working Hours)
For faculty members, a specialized discretionary labor system applies, and they are deemed to have worked 7 hours and 45 minutes per day. Even when online meetings are held late at night for international joint research or similar activities, applications for compensatory leave are permitted.

Article 10 (Leave)
In the first year, 20 days of annual paid leave are granted, as well as a summer shutdown (three consecutive days in mid-August) and refresh leave (five days for every 10 years of service). Special leave categories include volunteer activity leave, lay judge (jury duty) leave, and family care leave.

Article 15 (Prevention of Harassment)
All faculty and staff must complete annual harassment prevention training (e-learning). If a violation is confirmed, the matter will be referred to the Disciplinary Committee.
──────────────────────────────────────────
[Attached documents] Telework Guidelines, Business Travel Expense Regulations, Manual for the Prevention of Research Fund Misconduct (PDF attached). Alternatively, the full text of the Faculty Employment Regulations can be downloaded from the internal portal site “Information for Faculty and Staff.”

Guide to Applying for Grants-in-Aid for Scientific Research (KAKENHI) [Fundamental Research] for FY 2025 Call

1. Application period
   Late August to early October 2024 (via the electronic application system). Approval by the affiliated institution is due by 17:00 two days prior to the application deadline. Both the Principal Investigator and Co-Investigators must ensure their e-Rad IDs are valid.

2. Eligibility
   Researchers employed full-time (including tenure, tenure-track, and fixed-term with mandatory retirement) at national, public, or private universities, inter-university research institutes, national research and development agencies, and other public research institutions. Part-time researchers must submit a certificate of affiliation issued by the head of their institution.

3. Budget scale and duration
   (1) Fundamental Research (A): 50 million–200 million JPY / 3–5 years
   (2) Fundamental Research (B): 5 million–50 million JPY / 3–5 years
   (3) Fundamental Research (C): 2 million–5 million JPY / 3–5 years

4. Review categories and evaluation criteria
   ① Appropriateness of research objectives; ② Feasibility of the research plan; ③ Research achievements and potential; ④ Appropriateness of the research budget; ⑤ Expected research outcomes and ripple effects.

5. New items (FY 2025)
   - Mandatory submission of a Research Data Management Plan (DMP).
   - Introduction of a “Young Researcher Development Bonus”: +0.2 points for PIs under 40 years old.

Contact: Grants-in-Aid for Scientific Research Review Division I, Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS) (TEL 03-3263-XXXX). Depending on the inquiry method, response times may vary; please contact us well in advance.

The amounts and allowances shown here are model cases based on typical assumptions, and the actual annual salary is determined individually according to academic background, work history, performance, work location, and the results of personnel evaluations. The regional allowance is linked to the cost-of-living index and prices at the duty location, and year-end and diligence allowances are calculated based on the corporation’s work rules and performance evaluations. Housing and commuting allowances have specified caps and eligibility requirements, and practical details—such as the cost of public transportation passes, exceptions for long-distance commuting, and prorating based on the number of telework days—are handled in separate regulations. Research support funds may be budgeted in the first year of appointment as startup assistance and can be used for consumables, conference participation, and software licenses, but are often not available for capital expenditures or expenses related to side jobs. Where an incentive system tied to the acquisition of external funds exists, a portion of the overhead may be reinvested in laboratory operations and human resource development. Note that tax treatment, calculation of social insurance premiums, application of dependent allowances, and points to consider for year-end adjustments and final tax returns follow separate HR and accounting guidelines. In addition, salary levels may vary significantly depending on specific job duties and work location, so it is recommended to consult the HR office of each faculty or graduate school for details. These rules are highly ambiguous, and establishing more rigorous regulations to ensure fair evaluations remains a challenge. Discussions on revising the rules are currently underway.

Gyousai University Faculty Promotion and Advancement Evaluation Criteria
FY2024 Edition (Internal Document Excerpt)
──────────────────────────────────────────
1. Evaluation cycle
- Associate Professor → Professor: In principle, eligibility to apply is granted every 6 years (earliest in 4 years if performance standards are met).
- Lecturer → Associate Professor: In principle, every 5 years; tenure-track faculty must undergo a midterm review in the 3rd year.

2. Performance indicators
[Research] 10 or more SSCI/Scopus-indexed papers; at least 2 papers in Top 10% journals; average external funding of 20 million JPY per year.
[Teaching] 2 undergraduate courses + 1 graduate course per year; average student FD survey score of 4.2/5 or higher.
[Service/Outreach] At least 2 items selected from policy recommendation reports, media commentary, positions such as society board member/editorial board, etc.

3. Review process
- Internal document screening → external peer review by 3 reviewers → public mock lecture → faculty meeting vote. Passing threshold: approval by at least two-thirds of current professors.
──────────────────────────────────────────
Appendix: Self-assessment template, external review request forms (JP/EN), sample annual performance input manual (CSV). The evaluation cycle grants the right to apply at fixed intervals; early applications require high-level performance and external evaluations. While quantitative indicators are presented for the three pillars of research, teaching, and social contribution, qualitative assessment that reflects disciplinary characteristics (e.g., impact of publications, scale and role in projects, continuity of educational improvement, and effectiveness of policy recommendations) is also emphasized.

This guide summarizes the residency procedures for faculty members who have obtained degrees or have research experience at overseas universities when they take up a position at our university, covering every step from the preparation stage to before and after arrival in Japan. The main residency statuses assumed are “Professor,” intended primarily for educational and research activities, and “Highly Skilled Professional (i)(c),” which is a points-based status premised on advanced specialization. The choice between them depends on factors such as job duties, whether a spouse can work, and plans for future permanent residency applications. Applications are typically submitted by proxy to the Immigration Services Agency via the International Affairs Division, with the host department compiling documents such as the notice of employment offer or employment contract, degree verification and list of achievements, and a summary of the research plan. Standard processing takes several weeks, and additional inquiries may occur during peak periods. Ensure that the romanized spelling on identification perfectly matches the passport, and confirm early how former surnames and middle names will be handled. For accompanying family members, “Dependent” status is common for spouses and children, and requires a family registry (with English translation), proof of sufficient funds, and documentation of family relationships. Before arrival, we recommend arranging medical insurance suitable for long-term stays, preparing prescriptions and ingredient lists for any medications you will bring, and creating English copies of immunization records. After arrival, a series of procedures directly connected to daily life and work will follow, including issuance of a residence card, resident registration, receipt of a My Number, opening a bank account and setting up communications contracts, registering with J-Alert, and issuance of on-campus accounts. Managing re-entry permits and extensions of the period of stay is each individual’s responsibility; also check your passport’s remaining validity and any complex travel itineraries.

Faculty Housing and Housing Subsidy Handbook, Expanded Edition (Revised September 2024)

This handbook provides a comprehensive summary of the systems, procedures, and post-move-in considerations that support housing for faculty employed at our university. Faculty housing is designed to enhance commuting convenience and promote on-campus collaboration, and is available in multiple types ranging from 1LDK to 3LDK, including barrier-free units and layouts tailored to family composition. Eligibility is determined by factors such as position, years of service, and household income; when applications exceed availability, candidates are placed on a waiting list and offered units in order.

Rent is calculated based on internal university standards, and additional fees may apply for common area charges, bicycle parking, car parking, and internet service. Before moving in, you must obtain fire insurance, complete a joint guarantor procedure, receive the keys, and participate in a move-in inspection. Photographing and recording the condition of fixtures at the time of move-in can be helpful in determining restoration requirements at the time of move-out. DIY projects and redecorating require prior approval; structural changes such as wall mounting or plumbing modifications are generally not permitted. Pet ownership may be restricted by policy, and separate guidelines apply to assistance dogs.

The housing subsidy supports residence in off-campus rentals and is subject to a maximum amount and subsidy rate. Because the subsidy rate varies according to household requirements (number of dependents), please promptly report any changes in family composition.

[Faculty Achievement Management System (UT-Research) Operation Manual] Ver. 5.1 (Extended Edition)
──────────────────────────────────────────
UT-Research is a core internal system that centrally enables the registration, updating, and output of faculty achievements in research, education, and societal engagement. Login is protected by multi-factor authentication via SSO, and upon first login the affiliation department code and job category information are linked. Registration categories are diverse, including peer-reviewed papers, international conferences, books, patents, external funding, awards, social activities, and media appearances. Each record is assigned attributes such as role (PI/Co-I), peer-review status, language of the achievement, and identifiers such as DOI/ISBN/patent number. CSV batch uploads must be in UTF-8 and fields enclosed in double quotes; validation will return errors if required columns are missing or data types do not match. When using the ORCID integration, basic metadata can be imported while respecting visibility settings, but post-import cleanup is recommended because author order and corresponding author notation vary by publisher. Automatic metadata retrieval from DOIs may be affected by browser specification updates; temporary issues in specific versions are scheduled to be resolved via patch releases. To avoid duplicate registrations, a duplicate detection mechanism is implemented based on the combination of identical title, year, and identifier; when candidates are presented, you can choose to merge them or retain them as separate entries.

Faculty Childcare/Caregiving Leave and Tenure Clock Extension Policy (Expanded Version), Revised December 2023
──────────────────────────────────────────
This policy establishes leave systems related to childcare and caregiving, as well as adjustments to the timing of evaluations related to research performance (tenure clock extensions), to support work-life balance and the continuation of diverse careers. Childcare leave may be taken in multiple segments until the child reaches a certain age, allowing faculty to alternate with a partner or design leave periods to align with academic term schedules. Salaries and allowances are paid in stages through a combination of systems: full pay for a certain period, followed by benefits at a prescribed percentage; however, supplementary university rules ensure that bonus calculations and salary increases are handled in a way that avoids undue disadvantages. Caregiving leave sets a cap on the total number of days per eligible family member, and, to address situations requiring short-term intensive care, flexible use in half-day or hourly units may be permitted in some cases. Tenure clock extensions allow individual postponement of evaluation deadlines when leave or similar circumstances exceed a specified number of days; based on the individual’s application, the Personnel Committee reviews the request together with a statement from the department head. Because extensions are not granted automatically, attaching materials that outline the impact on research and teaching plans facilitates the decision. Special consideration is also given through individual review to circumstances outside typical cases, such as multiple births, adoption, and long-term medical treatment.

The research ethics education program consists of required and elective courses that systematically teach foundational literacy to ensure research reliability and reproducibility. Required courses include Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) and data management, while electives cover AI ethics, understanding bias, ethics of human and animal experimentation, and basics of collaborative research agreements, and are offered through an appropriate combination of online and in-person formats. Within the Learning Management System (LMS), course registration, progress tracking, quizzes, and certificate issuance are all completed, and achievement is assessed through end-of-chapter quizzes and short written case-study assignments. A quantitatively defined passing threshold is set, and multiple retake opportunities are provided; however, if the program is not completed within the enrollment period, applications for and acceptance procedures of external funding may be restricted, so caution is advised. If equivalent content has been completed in an externally accredited course such as CITI Japan, alternative certification can be granted through prescribed procedures, requiring submission of the latest certificate and syllabus. The program goes beyond mere legal compliance to address practice-oriented issues such as promoting open science, balancing data sharing and personal data protection, copyright and privacy issues associated with the use of generative AI, requirements for the retention of research notebooks and electronic lab records, secondary use of figures and tables, and the handling of preprints. It also outlines the basics of Material Transfer Agreements (MTAs) and Data Transfer Agreements (DTAs), which are common in international collaborative research, and teaches methods for classifying confidential information and managing access permissions.

[Concurrent Appointments and Conflict of Interest Management Regulations] Enforced April 2024 (Expanded Edition)
──────────────────────────────────────────
These regulations provide a framework that both promotes faculty engagement with society to exercise their expertise and ensures neutrality within the university and fairness in student supervision. Concurrent appointments include a variety of forms such as unpaid academic society positions, lectures, corporate advisory roles, and startup executive or CTO positions. The content of activities, compensation, and time commitment must be clarified, and prior notification or approval is required. In particular, director compensation or stock option grants from companies will be reviewed by the Conflict of Interest Committee in light of their relevance to research topics and the involvement of students. An annual cap is set on total compensation, and equity compensation must be declared at market value. As a rule, licensing agreements for research outcomes are conducted through the university’s Technology Licensing Organization (TLO), and direct contracts by individuals should be avoided. When involving students in projects at the concurrent employer, a third-party mentor must be assigned to ensure fairness in supervision, with clear delineation between evaluation and compensation. FCOI (Financial Conflict of Interest) disclosures related to public research funds follow separate forms, and an annual report on concurrent appointments and conflicts of interest is required at the end of the fiscal year. If undisclosed activities or omissions in declarations come to light, measures such as a ban on future concurrent appointments or salary reductions may be imposed.

Guidelines for Subsidies on Research Equipment Relocation and Moving Expenses Upon Transfer In 2025-02 Public Notice (Expanded Edition)
──────────────────────────────────────────
These guidelines establish a framework to support the rapid launch of research activities by faculty newly hired and relocating to the University from distant areas, by subsidizing basic moving costs and the transportation and installation costs of research equipment. Eligible applicants are full-time professors and associate professors, and the scope of subsidies is determined based on relocation distance, employment status, and the nature of the research equipment. Basic moving costs include domestic transport of household goods, books, and research materials, and optional additional fees may apply for packing items such as pianos, large bookshelves, and precision instruments. For research equipment relocation, the scope assumes heavy and precision machines such as clean benches, vacuum systems, NMRs, and 3D printers; quotations must be obtained from multiple specialized vendors for comparison. A pre-survey of the delivery route and confirmation of requirements for floor load capacity, power supply capacity, exhaust systems, and safety management are mandatory, with scheduling coordinated with the University’s safety, health, and facilities divisions. Ocean freight from overseas is subject to a separate review; customs brokerage fees, taxes and fees upon import, and insurance coverage must be clearly defined. Subsidy applications must be submitted to the Research Promotion Office at an appropriate time after issuance of the employment offer, using the prescribed forms and attaching quotations. As a rule, disbursements are made on an actual cost basis within the budget allocation; attention must be paid to the distinction between personal belongings and research equipment, as well as to criteria for useful life and asset capitalization. Applicants must also confirm insurance enrollment to cover damage or loss during transport, the handling of costs for reinstallation and calibration of equipment, and procedures for industrial waste disposal associated with discarding unneeded equipment.

question: At the Reikou Exchange, during the period from April 1, 2024 to March 31, 2025, which day of the week most frequently falls on the last trading day for the Marusa & Ixsa 3-month interest rate futures monthly contracts?

negative_chunk_list.content:
[Contract Period and Rollover Provisions]
Article 5: Contract Month Trading Period and Renewal Method
1. Trading in Marusa & Ixsa 3-Month Interest Rate Futures follows a “22 contract months system,” consisting of seven quarterly months (March, June, September, December) and fifteen serial months (all other months). Quarterly months are listed up to five years ahead, and serial months up to three years ahead.
2. This Article sets the final trading day as “the business day preceding the maturity date” or a date separately determined by the President, without explicitly specifying weekday-based criteria. Terms such as “third Wednesday” or “two business days prior” do not appear and are delegated to the Rollover Operational Standards (separate volume).
3. The recommended timing for the roll calendar is the second Wednesday of the month preceding maturity, but market participants may, at their discretion, roll earlier. It is explicitly stated that if the roll ratio into quarterly months falls below 70%, the Exchange may require additional margin, indicating a liquidity constraint unrelated to weekday distribution.
4. The President may advance the final trading day on an ad hoc basis during monetary policy events (policy rate changes, special official discount rate measures, etc.) or periods of heightened geopolitical risk. As an example of discretionary action, it cites the emergency measures on March 13, 2020, during COVID-19, when the scheduled Monday final trading day was moved up to Friday.
5. The appendix to this Article includes a “Recommended Roll Spread Formula (IMM forward curve differential method)” and a “Roll Calendar Quick Reference (with Excel macro),” but both are information on a different dimension from weekday logic and cannot serve as a basis for weekday statistics.

[Unsecured Call O/N Futures Last Trading Day Rules]
Article 10 Special provisions and postponements on the last trading day
1. For this product, the last trading day is defined as the business day following the third Thursday of each contract month. If a Japanese public holiday falls on the third Thursday, it is postponed to Friday; if Friday is also a holiday, it is sequentially postponed to the following Monday.
2. If the Tandon market is closed for a bank holiday, in order to avoid EUCR settlement congestion, the last trading day may be postponed to the final business day of that week (typically Wednesday). The documentation includes, as a specific example, the handling of the summer bank holiday in the week of August 26, 2024.
3. If a massive NOC (Net-Of-Coupon) settlement shortfall is anticipated, the Chair retains discretion to further postpone the last trading day to “the business day following the fourth Thursday.” As a result, there were two historical instances in 2022 where the last trading day for the same contract month was changed up to three times.
4. In this adjustment process, “two business days before the third Wednesday” is not referenced, and the weekday pattern varies from Thursday → Friday → Monday → Wednesday. Therefore, confusing this with the statistics for the Marsa & Ixsa 3M futures can be a significant source of error. Against this backdrop, the last trading day for Unsecured Call O/N Futures is unsuitable as material for weekday statistics. None of the provisions in this chapter has the effect of defining or altering the “last trading day itself” for the Marsa & Ixsa three-month interest rate futures, nor can they be used as grounds for drawing statistical conclusions regarding weekday distributions.

[Contract Month Position Limits and Clearing Framework] (Extended Version)
──────────────────────────────────────────
This section defines position limits, clearing units, margin calculation, and the operational flow of clearing. Position limit management for the Marsa & Ixa 3‑month interest rate futures is the core of risk governance, designed to curb excessive one-sided positioning in the market and ensure the resilience of the central counterparty (CCP). Sub-caps on quarterly contract months are introduced to smooth spikes in clearing load caused by near-dated concentration, and additional margin upon limit breaches is collected immediately based on notional principal and a risk weight (e.g., 3 bp). Clearing is, in principle, by cash settlement, with variation between the trade price and settlement price settled on the business day following the last trading day; the final settlement price is fixed by a trigger based on a pre-published formula. Eligible collateral includes cash, government bonds, and high-grade corporate bonds, with haircuts set in tiers according to volatility and liquidity. Details of operations—such as the distinction between intraday and overnight risk, deadlines for interday variation margin (VM) calls, and conditions for activating intraday extraordinary calls—are consolidated here. Crucially, this chapter aims at “certainty of clearing and funding safety” and does not address any logic for determining weekdays or the identification of the final day. The fact that cutoffs and collection times are fixed does not provide any indication of “which weekdays the final day tends to fall on.”

[Settlement Date and Bank Holiday Calculation] (Extended Version)
──────────────────────────────────────────
This clause sets out the scheduling rules for the “settlement date (the date on which payments and receipts are executed)” and serves a different role from the definition of the final trading date itself. The settlement date is determined based on a specified business day within the week (for example, the fourth business day), using an algorithm that sequentially defers the date in accordance with domestic and international bank holidays and the arrangement of consecutive holidays. During weeks that include extended holidays such as Golden Week or the year-end/New Year period, or that include substitute holidays, a maximum adjustment range in business days is permitted to avoid settlement congestion, thereby preventing bottlenecks or errors in fund transfers. Furthermore, provisions granting a compensatory interest rate for the time value of settlement funds (e.g., O/N swap plus a fixed basis) are operationally important to ensure fairness among participants, but they do not answer the question of “when to cease trading.” The “business day count” within the clause is a technical mechanism to absorb the impact of banking holidays and is a concept at a different level from product-specific final-day logics such as “the third Wednesday” or “two business days prior.” Therefore, it is inappropriate to infer, based on this clause, conclusions such as “the final day tends to fall closer to Thursday.” This clause addresses how dates for settlements (clearing/delivery) associated with a finalized final day are adjusted, and mixing it into the population used for analyzing weekday frequencies leads to a shifting of the issue.

[Emergency Trading Halt Measures]
Article 13: Market Suspension in the Event of Natural Disasters or Cyberattacks
1. In the event of occurrences that impair market continuity—such as earthquakes of lower 6 on the seismic intensity scale or higher, typhoons with maximum instantaneous wind speeds exceeding 50 m/s, tsunami warnings, loss of satellite communication networks, or nationwide cyberattacks—the Exchange may immediately suspend all interest rate futures trading upon consultation between the Executive Director and the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO). The suspension decision will be disseminated as “Code Red” via the internal communication network and simultaneously to major ISVs.

2. In principle, a temporary lifting will be attempted by 15:00 on the same day; if recovery is not possible, an official announcement designating an “Emergency Suspension Day” will be made by 16:30. All trades on that day will be treated as canceled, and margins will be fixed at the prior business day’s settlement price. During the recovery period, real-time risk measurement will fail over to the backup center (Bànxī).

3. If the Emergency Suspension Day falls on a weekend or public holiday, the next business day will become the “Supplementary Final Trading Day.” This ad hoc date will overwrite the official calendar and take precedence over the original “two business days before the third Wednesday” logic for Marusa & Ixsa 3M futures, thereby introducing a factor that may distort weekday statistics.

4. If the suspension exceeds 48 hours, the Chairperson may declare a “roll of all positions to the next contract month.” The roll reference date shall be within two business days after the suspension is lifted, and the new final trading day generated after the forced roll shall follow the date specific to the destination contract month. Consequently, multiple final trading days may exist within the same contract month before and after the suspension.

[Extended Trading Hours Extension Guidelines]
──────────────────────────────────────────
We have established a mechanism that automatically extends the night session when market integrity indicators—such as volume, spread, and fill rate—exceed certain thresholds. The extension is a technical operation intended to enhance participant convenience and promote price discovery during event-risk windows. The decision to extend is announced at a specified time, and related processes—such as clearing cutoffs and collateral transfer deadlines—are correspondingly pushed back.

Note that “trading day” is treated as a single unit based on a session regime even if it crosses calendar days. For example, trading from Monday 23:00 to Tuesday 05:00 is considered “Monday trading,” which introduces temporal handling of this kind. In manual aggregation and external reporting, there is a risk of misidentifying the day of the week due to adherence to calendar changes; if one naively feeds the calendar dates of the extended session into day-of-week frequency statistics, the proportions for Monday and Tuesday will become distorted.

That said, these extension guidelines do not prescribe which day of the week is to be treated as the “final day.” Even when the final day overlaps with an extended session, the final day itself is independently defined in the product specifications; this guide is merely a technical rule for extending the execution window. Therefore, it is inappropriate to use the descriptions in this guide to determine the modal day of the week. Rather, when aggregating, one must strictly distinguish between sessions and calendar days.

[Clearing System Failure Response Procedures]
Article 15 Provisional Clearing in the Event of a Failure
1. When the clearing system (Settlement Gateway) detects a failure, the Exchange declares “Code Amber” and fixes the provisional clearing price (provisional settlement value) at either the previous business day’s closing price or the mid-price of three primary dealers designated by the President. Margin calculation enters “Freeze Mode,” under which only the maintenance margin is maintained.
2. If the failure persists for more than 48 hours, the status is escalated to “Code Red,” and the President triggers a “forced rollover.” Positions in the affected contract month, including arbitrage trades, are transferred in bulk to the next quarterly contract month, and the original last trading day is treated as lapsed. Because the calendar of the destination contract month defines the new last trading day, the “two business days before the third Wednesday” principle is temporarily suspended, breaking weekday-based statistics.
3. Discrepancies between the provisional clearing price and the official recalculated price are evaluated with a fair value adjustment based on “σ(30),” the most recent 30-day volatility. If the mismatch exceeds ±2% (200 bp), additional margin (Extra Margin) is required in two tiers (5% and 10%). If the failure month falls near month-end, clearing procedures are carried over to the following month, amplifying weekday errors across the month boundary.
4. The footnotes discuss two cases: the approach of Typhoon No. 19 in October 2019 and a large-scale DDoS attack in July 2023. In both cases, the last trading day was replaced by the provisional clearing day, serving as cautionary examples of significantly distorted weekday frequencies in official statistics, such as Monday shifting to Tuesday and Friday shifting to Monday.
5. The annex includes detailed operational materials such as the failure-response flowchart, linked automatic shutdown logic, and procedures for margin settlement outside bank counter business hours.

[Hedge Accounting Application Case Handbook]
Article 16: Accounting Treatment Examples and Period-End Measurement
1. This handbook cross-references Accounting Standard No. 28 and IFRS 9 “Financial Instruments,” providing a detailed practical explanation for cases where Marusa & Ixsa three-month interest rate futures are designated as a cash flow hedge. It adopts a typical example in which the hedged item is 3M TIBOR variable cash flows and the hedging instrument is Marusa & Ixsa 3M futures (short position).
2. Hedge effectiveness is assessed using the “dollar-offset method” and the “regression analysis method.” In the former, a hedge ratio of 80%–125% is set as the qualifying range; in the latter, R² ≥ 0.8 is set as the qualifying threshold. Practical examples list the assessment date as “period-end or the second Friday during the period,” which does not match the market rule of “two business days before the third Wednesday.”
3. Journal entry examples:
   (1) When a valuation gain arises (hedging instrument shows a valuation loss)
       Dr: Other comprehensive income 10,000,000 yen / Cr: Derivatives gains and losses 10,000,000 yen
   (2) Reclassification adjustment (hedge de-designation)
       Dr: Non-operating expenses 10,000,000 yen / Cr: Other comprehensive income 10,000,000 yen
4. The internal control audit worksheet presents “exceptional journal entries when the assessment date coincides with Friday or the month-end business day,” noting that weekday-based criteria vary widely, such as “Friday,” “month-end,” and “the 15th business day.”

[Risk Management Committee Report]
Article 17: Risk Indicators and Monitoring Framework
1. The Committee monitors interest rate derivatives as a whole, including Marusa & Ikusa 3-month interest rate futures, using (i) Parametric VaR (observation window: 250 business days; holding period: 10 days; confidence level: 99%), (ii) Historical VaR (observation window: 750 business days; holding period: 10 days), and (iii) Stress Scenario VaR (LTCM, Lehman replay, pandemic shock). Measurement results are finalized in draft on the second business day of each month and approved on the third business day.
2. The “Market Events Calendar” section of the Committee report lists contract last trading days, but the purpose is to use them as explanatory variables for a liquidity metric (Amihud illiquidity), not to conduct weekday analysis. Stress recalculation dates are dispersed across “first Monday,” “third Tuesday,” “the business day following quarter-end,” etc., and are unrelated to the “two business days before the third Wednesday” logic.
3. The report is in dashboard format, with PFE (Potential Future Exposure) and EPE (Expected Positive Exposure) also displayed as graphs. All screenshot examples are created as of Tuesday 17:00, which may risk introducing weekday bias. Therefore, the days on which Committee materials are prepared or reviewed are not assumed to have any statistical impact on the last trading weekday of the 3M futures.

[Notes on Interest Rate Futures in Securities Reports] (Expanded Version)
──────────────────────────────────────────
The statutory disclosure notes prepared by issuers—such as “ending balance,” “average balance,” “fair value,” “amount pledged as collateral,” and “application of hedge accounting”—are intended to satisfy financial reporting requirements and are not explanatory manuals for “last-day logic” under exchange rules. Accounting technical rules such as carrying forward to the next business day when the period-end falls on a weekend or holiday, and the measurement date used in notes (period-end/preceding business day), are designed to prioritize comparability in disclosures and ease of audit. As a result, the points in time used within the notes tend to cluster on Fridays or month-end business days; however, this is due to “efficiency in disclosure operations” and “standardization of audit processes,” and does not indicate any weekday bias in the final trading day of futures. Furthermore, supplemental disclosures of moving average balances (e.g., the 10 business days prior to period-end) are statistical materials showing seasonality in positions and are separate from the definition of the final day. Therefore, it is misguided to infer from statutory disclosure note examples that “this product often ends on Fridays.” The determination of the final day is made strictly in accordance with the product-specific rules, and the disclosure notes merely describe the resulting balances and values.

[Fee and Market Maker Program Guide] Revised October 2024 (Overview)
──────────────────────────────────────────
This guide summarizes the trading fee structure, incentive design, and Market Maker (hereafter, MM) program operating rules for interest rate derivatives on the Reikou Exchange. Covered products include Marusa & Ikusa 3-month interest rate futures, JPY interest rate swap futures by tenor, and short-term rate-related options. This document focuses on the framework for price formation and liquidity provision and does not address logic relating to each product’s last trading day or start date. Participants must consult the product specifications and agreements before trading.

■ Fee structure (Maker/Taker model)
  1. Exchange fees consist of maker rebates (for providing liquidity) and taker fees (for consuming liquidity), depending on the execution side. The standard rate is adjusted in tiers by volume, with a taker fee of X yen per contract and a maker rebate of up to −Y yen per contract.
  2. Clearing costs (settlement-related) are charged separately and allocated in relation to the number of daily variation margin (VM) flows and haircut levels by collateral asset class. When thresholds are exceeded within a month, the next tier is automatically applied from the following business day, and the status resets at month-end.
  3. Block discounts apply when off-order-book trades (blocks) are reported to and cleared through the central order book. Reporting deadlines, minimum sizes, and permissible price deviations are set per product; violations revert to the standard rate plus an added penalty.

negative_chunk_list.content:
Smartest Electric Store Information

Smartest Electric is a large specialty store where you can enjoy choosing everything from home appliances and PCs to lifestyle goods, food, and books—all on one floor. If this is your first visit, please pick up a floor map at the information counter to the right of the entrance. The store is divided into zones such as “Home Appliances,” “Computers & Peripherals,” “Kitchen,” “Lifestyle,” “Food,” and “Books,” with numerous displays that let you compare and try items across categories.

Business Hours and Crowd Guide

  • Weekdays: 10:00–20:00 / Weekends & Holidays: 9:00–21:00
  • It’s relatively less crowded right after opening and in the late afternoon onward.

Membership Services

Register for the free “Smartest Members” program to enjoy benefits such as receipt management via the app, discounts on extended warranties, and increased reward point rates. Registering before you shop will make checkout smoother.

Reservations and Special Orders

If you can’t find the product you’re looking for in the store, please speak to a staff member. We can check warehouse inventory and arrange special orders. Availability periods and methods may vary depending on the product type and stock status.

Trial and Experience Corners

In the main categories, we have experience corners where you can try out actual usability. You can check keyboard typing feel, screen visibility, the operability of kitchen appliances, differences in beverage flavors, and more.

Note: Prices, displays, and expected arrivals vary by store and date. For the latest availability, please check with in-store staff or the official app.

First-Time PC Buying Guide (Smartest Denki)

When choosing a computer, the key is to identify specifications that match your intended use. For tasks like writing reports and browsing, opt for a lightweight mobile model; for image editing or 3D work, choose a machine with a high-performance GPU. Narrow down your options based on your purpose.

Main specs to check

  • CPU: Check the balance of generation, core count, and power consumption. Energy-efficient models tend to offer longer battery life.
  • Memory: For smooth performance in everyday use, having extra capacity helps with running multiple apps simultaneously.
  • Storage: SSDs boot quickly, and NVMe drives provide an even more noticeable speed boost. If you have many photos or videos, prioritize capacity.
  • Display: Check resolution, color accuracy, and low reflectivity. Brightness is also important if you use it outdoors.
  • I/O ports: Connectivity is easy to overlook—check video outputs, USB generations, and whether there’s a card reader.

Points to consider before purchase

If you carry your PC frequently, also check weight, chassis material, and hinge durability. For the keyboard layout and touchpad feel, hands-on testing is important.

Note: Listed specifications and model lineups are updated periodically. Availability and handling of individual models vary by store, so our staff will provide the latest information.

Basics of Microwave Ovens and How to Choose (Smartest Electric)

A microwave oven is a home appliance that heats food quickly by vibrating water molecules inside the ingredients with microwaves. Recently, models that automatically adjust heating using sensors and multifunction models that also offer oven and grill features have become available. Since the interior capacity directly affects usage, the selection criteria will differ depending on whether you primarily reheat boxed lunches or plan to bake bread and use larger plates.

Main features

  • Auto programs: Reduce overcooking and uneven heating, directly improving everyday convenience.
  • Inverter control: Precisely controls output to improve quality during defrosting.
  • Steam/superheated steam: Useful for reheating fried foods and healthy cooking.
  • Ease of cleaning: Flat cavities, deodorizing functions, and coatings affect how easy it is to clean.

For safe use

Avoid plastics without heat-resistance markings and dishes with metallic decorations, and always loosen the lid of sealed containers. After heating, the interior and dishes will be very hot, so use oven mitts. By following the safety precautions in the instruction manual, you can cook comfortably and safely.

Information: If you would like demonstrations of display models or feature comparisons, staff in the kitchen appliances section will explain how to operate them. The lineup is updated as needed depending on the season and manufacturers’ new product releases.

Make Instant Coffee Even Tastier (Smartest Electric Food & Cafe)

The appeal of instant coffee is that you can enjoy it easily by simply dissolving it in hot water. In general, there are freeze-dried and spray-dried methods, which result in differences in grain shape, solubility, and aroma. Put the appropriate amount in a mug, “loosen” it with a small amount of hot water first, then add hot water to your preferred strength to stabilize the flavor.

Tips for Variations

  • Adding milk or plant-based milk makes the mouthfeel smoother.
  • When making it iced with ice cubes, dissolve it strong first so it won’t get diluted as easily.
  • A small amount of cinnamon, cocoa powder, or honey gives it a dessert-like touch.

Storage and Use-Up Tips

After opening, avoid humidity and high temperatures, and store with the lid tightly closed. If you want to enjoy the aroma, try to use it up sooner. Following the best-before date and storage instructions on the package will help maintain quality.

In-store Support: Our staff will guide you through flavor differences and recommended variations. For tasting event information, please check the Events section of the official app. We also carry coffee equipment and goods, so please have a look. We look forward to your visit.

Inventory Management Policy and Replenishment Cycle (Internal Guide)

This page is an internal document summarizing the design principles for appropriate inventory levels (PAR values) at each sales floor of Smartest Electric, along with daily replenishment cycles and rounding rules for order lots. SKUs are classified into three quadrants: best sellers, semi-best sellers, and long-term inventory. Demand forecasting is based on exponential smoothing with a smoothing factor of 0.35 using sales from the most recent 13 weeks. When demand spikes due to sales or campaigns, the marketing team will distribute a demand factor (ranging from 1.1 to 2.5) at least 72 hours in advance, which will be reflected in order proposals. To prevent overstocking, PAR values are adjusted by a seasonal index (Spring=0.95, Summer=1.10, Autumn=1.00, Winter=1.05), and for categories where stockout costs are considered high, safety stock is calculated based on a 95% service level.

Replenishment days are set according to the characteristics of each category, and backroom inventory counts are conducted after closing on the last business day of the month. Inbound inspections involve scanning JAN/RFID with handheld terminals and registering items by condition category (good, crushed box, requires operational check). As a rule, B-grade items such as crushed-box or returned display units are transferred to the outlet shelf rather than regular shelves. For food products, FEFO (First-Expired, First-Out) must be strictly followed, and sales floor staff ensure visibility through front-facing replenishment.

Category | Replenishment Days | PAR Value Basis | Safety Stock Calculation | Order Lot
Refrigerators & Large White Goods | Tue, Fri | Weekly average sales × 2.4 | Demand distribution × 95% SL | Units of 1
Vacuum Cleaners & Small Air Appliances | Mon, Thu, Sat | Weekly average sales × 1.8 | Considering daily variability coefficient | 2–4 units
Rice Cookers & Kettles | Wed, Sat

Inter-Store Inventory Transfer and Approval Flow (Operational Procedures)

This document outlines the standard procedures and SLAs (Service Level Agreements) for conducting inventory transfers (store transfers) between stores. As a rule, transfer requests to fulfill customer reservations are prioritized. Requests should be submitted from the POS hold screen or from the WMS “Transfer Instruction” menu, and inventory allocation is made from the shipping store’s backroom stock. Sales floor display items are not eligible for transfer, but may be transferred as Grade B items with manager approval.

Requests are managed under three priority levels, with vehicle arrangements and picking cutoffs varying by urgency. Normally, transfers are shipped in the next business day’s midday run, but during peak periods, there are three runs: morning, midday, and evening. All transfer items must have a transfer ticket (with barcode) attached, and the receiving store will match labels during inbound inspection. If counts do not match, a discrepancy report must be filed immediately, and root-cause analysis must begin the same day.

Priority | Expected Scenario | Cutoff Time | Delivery Run | SLA
High (A) | Day before reservation due date; gift deadline | 11:00 same day | Special run/Direct ship | Same day to next morning
Medium (B) | Replenishing sales floor stockouts | 17:00 previous day | Next-day midday run | By 14:00 next day
Low (C) | Planogram adjustments; slow-moving inventory cleanup | As needed | Regular consolidated run | 2–3 business days

Costs associated with transfers are provisionally charged to the shipping store in accordance with internal allocation rules. Settlements are processed monthly, and amounts are reallocated to the destination store’s budget. Use of special runs is generally prohibited; in emergencies, obtain prior approval from the area manager.

2025 Summer Sale Operations Guide (Discounts, Points, Coupons)

This page explains discount rules, point rewards, and the order of coupon application during the summer promotion period. Discounts are basically capped by category, and whether they can be combined with manufacturer promotions is set in the master at the SKU level.

Category | Maximum Discount | Weekend Points Bonus | Combinability | Notes
- Refrigerators & Washing Machines | 15% | +3% | Can be combined with manufacturer discounts | Delivery and installation charged separately
- TVs & Video Equipment | 12% | +4% | Some non-combinable | +1% with extended warranty enrollment
- Vacuums & Air Purifiers | 18% | +2% | Mostly combinable | Discontinued inventory handled separately
- Kitchen Accessories | 20% | +5% | Coupons combinable | Includes heat-resistant containers, etc.
- Lighting & Batteries | 10% | +2% | Some exclusions | Bulk purchase benefits available
- Food & Seasonings | 8% | +1% | Coupons combinable | Near-expiry items have separate discounts

Use the unified template for sale POPs, and to prevent price mislabeling, link them with the master by 23:00 on the day before the price revision. Returns and exchanges follow the standard rules, but items with special markdowns for inventory clearance may be final sale and non-returnable. Ensure strict compliance with the Act against Unjustifiable Premiums and Misleading Representations and the Act on Specified Commercial Transactions; manage display periods to avoid misleading dual pricing. Gift wrapping tends to cause wait times during peak hours, so distribute numbered tickets to alleviate congestion.

Section: Online Order to Pickup/Delivery Workflow (Click & Collect / Delivery)

This page explains the operational flow from ordering on the e-commerce site to in-store pickup or delivery. When a customer confirms an order, the OMS performs payment authorization and inventory allocation. For in-store pickup, a picking task is automatically issued to the nearest store. After picking is completed, the items are placed in a storage locker, and a readiness notification is sent via SMS/email. The pickup deadline is 72 hours from the notification; if it expires, the order is automatically canceled or a switch to delivery is proposed. For delivery, orders placed by the cutoff time ship the same day; orders placed after the cutoff ship on the next business day.

Area | Service Type | Cutoff | Standard Arrival | Notes
- Nearby prefecture | Regular service | 14:00 | Next day | Time slot selection available
- Major cities nationwide | Air mixed cargo | 12:00 | Next day to the day after next | Excludes large items
- Remote islands/mountain areas | Ferry/ground transport | 10:00 | 2–5 days | Subject to weather conditions
- Same-day urgent | Motorcycle courier | 11:00 | Same-day evening | Small items only

We also support gift designation and holding items during extended absence. For recipient verification, we ask for the order number and an ID. For the food category, cold-chain management is maintained even for in-store handoff to preserve temperature. After shipment, a tracking number is automatically issued, and customers can check delivery status on My Page. If delays are expected, we notify customers in advance and offer alternatives or reward points to prevent declines in customer satisfaction.

Returns, Exchanges, and Warranty Policy (2025 Edition)

As a general rule, returns and exchanges are accepted within 14 days if the item is unopened/unused and you present a receipt or purchase history. Initial defects are handled in accordance with the manufacturer’s warranty. Customers enrolled in our extended warranty (additional fee) receive prioritized repair/replacement services during the coverage period. If any accessories are missing, we may offer a reduced refund or decline the exchange. For hygiene products, consumables, and certain foods, returns are not accepted after opening due to hygiene and quality considerations.

Product Category | Initial Defect Acceptance | Extended Warranty | Required Documents
- Large Appliances | Within 7 days of purchase | Up to 5 years | Receipt/Warranty Card
- Small Appliances | Within 14 days of purchase | Up to 3 years | Receipt/Warranty Card
- Peripheral Accessories | Within 14 days of purchase | Some items excluded | Receipt
- Lighting/Batteries | Within 14 days of purchase | Some items excluded | Receipt
- Food/Seasonings | Same day only for exterior defects | Not available | Receipt

For repair intake, we verify symptom reproducibility and ask about the usage environment. If water damage or drops are suspected, charges may apply. Long-term warranties cover natural failures and do not include consumable replacements or cleaning. If an identical model is unavailable for replacement, we will propose an equivalent alternative or a refund capped at the purchase amount. Details on the cancellation policy and return shipping methods are provided in My Page.

question: How much are the overseas profits for Eco & Frontier’s Home Appliances, IoT Devices, Wearable devisons in the third quarter, respectively?
answer: Unable to answer because the necessary information sources do not exist.

negative_chunk_list.content:
Section: Accounting Policies for Segment Approach and Profit Classification (Effective FY2025)

This section is an internal guide that organizes the measurement methods for division-level performance at Eco & Frontier (Home Appliances, IoT Devices, Wearables) and, in particular, the accounting treatment of the “Overseas Profit” metric. “Overseas Profit” is defined, on a consolidated basis and by regional classification, as an operating-profit-equivalent concept calculated by deducting cost of goods sold, selling, general and administrative expenses, and research and development expenses (including allocations) from sales generated outside Japan. The results of overseas subsidiaries included in the scope of consolidation are presented after elimination of intercompany transactions, and the inclusion of equity-method affiliates is treated on a non-operating line. When the place of production and the place of sale differ, regional classification is, in principle, based on the “customer location.”

Profit levels are bridged from management accounting to financial accounting to align with the segment disclosure requirements of IFRS 8. Inter-segment transactions (e.g., the IoT Devices division supplying communication modules to the Home Appliances division) are priced with reference to the arm’s length principle and eliminated at the consolidation stage. SG&A allocation employs a hybrid approach using multiple drivers such as sales, units, and labor hours, and shared services costs (legal, HR, IT) are apportioned to divisions using allocation keys. Lease assets are recognized in accordance with IFRS 16, with depreciation and interest equivalents reflected in profit calculations.

Operational Guide to Foreign Currency Translation and Hedge Accounting (Approach to Overseas Profit Impact)

Fluctuations in overseas profits include translation effects from exchange rates in addition to actual fluctuations in local-currency results. This guide organizes the standard translation process when the reporting currency is JPY and the hedging policy to preserve economic substance. Revenue and expenses are translated at the quarterly average rate, and monetary balance sheet items are measured at the period-end rate. Foreign exchange gains and losses are recognized as non-operating income/expense or in other comprehensive income depending on their nature, and in practice, transaction hedges (forwards, options) are used in line with operating rules.

Hedge targets are net exposures in major currencies, and hedge accounting can be applied to forecasted transactions to the extent they meet internal criteria. For IoT devices and wearables, a mix of imported components and local sales produces partial natural hedges. In home appliances, while localization of installation and logistics costs is progressing, core components such as semiconductors and compressors are priced in USD, making USD/JPY fluctuations prone to affect gross margin.

Item | Translation Rate | Accounting Treatment | Notes
- Revenue/Expenses | Quarterly average | Recognized in P&L | Affects overseas profit
- Monetary assets and liabilities | Period-end rate | FX gains/losses | Classified as non-operating/OCI
- Forwards/Options | Contract rate | Hedge effectiveness assessment | Designation documentation required

How to Read Quarterly Reports: Merged Cells (rowspan/colspan) and Notation Rules

Our division-level quarterly tables use merged cells (rowspan/colspan) to improve readability, integrating division names and metric headers both vertically and horizontally. To avoid confusion when viewing, it is important to correctly understand the correspondence between the header hierarchy and the data. For example, “Sales/Profit” branches into subheaders of “Domestic/Overseas/Total,” which are then expanded across the rows for each quarter. Note that cell merging is solely for visual space-saving and does not affect the aggregation formulas or the meaning of the figures.

In practice, we standardize notation under the following rules: 1) Quarters are labeled “Q1–Q4”; 2) the monetary unit (e.g., millions of yen) is specified at the top right of the table; 3) regions are divided into “Domestic” and “Overseas”; 4) profit is based on the management KPI equivalent to operating profit. Because cell merging may cause a division name to span four rows (Q1–Q4), if a screenshot is clipped mid-row, it becomes easy to lose the correspondence of metrics; therefore, we recommend citing the entire table as the source.

Example reference format
- Header: (Example) Reference Format | Domestic (Sales, Profit) | Overseas (Sales, Profit)
- Rows:
  - Reference Division A | — — | — —
  - Reference Division A (continued) | — — | — —

Driver Analysis of Regional Margins (Conceptual Framework)

Fluctuations in overseas profits stem from a multi-layered cost and pricing determination process. Selling prices are influenced by local competitive dynamics, sales channels (e-commerce/mass retail/direct), promotion intensity, and the presence or absence of ancillary services such as maintenance and installation. Conversely, cost of goods is affected by component costs, utilization rates at manufacturing sites, logistics expenses (ocean/air/local delivery), and quality-related costs (returns/warranty). IoT devices incur ongoing operational costs for software updates and cloud connectivity, while wearables tend to have relatively larger allocations of R&D expenses aimed at improving sensor accuracy and extending battery life.

- Price factors: local competition, premium/mid-range mix ratio, impact of promotions and coupons.
- Cost factors: raw material indices, yield, degree of logistics tightness, maintenance parts procurement.
- Operational factors: sales staff training, after-sales service SLA, return rate, utilization rate.
- Regulatory factors: national energy efficiency regulations, wireless certifications, data privacy compliance costs.

To decompose these factors, managerial accounting employs a margin bridge analysis with blocks for price, mix, volume, COGS, promotions, logistics, R&D allocation, and FX. Analyses are conducted quarterly, complemented by visualizing core trends excluding one-off factors (major recall responses, natural disasters, supplier disruptions) to derive insights that inform management decisions. Each department reconciles findings with competitive price tracking and promotion calendars collected from overseas sales companies and presents improvement actions during KPI reviews.

Internal Transactions, Royalties, and Allocation Rules (Inter-segment Alignment)

In a global development, manufacturing, and sales structure, cross-functional internal transactions are unavoidable. Examples include cases where communication modules developed by the IoT devices division are adopted by home appliances and wearables, or where a common cloud platform is used. To ensure transparency and tax compliance, these internal charges apply pricing policies based on functions, risks, and assets. Software licenses and patent royalties are recorded in divisions at rates based on sales or cost, and are eliminated on consolidation.

Internal Transaction Type | Pricing Basis | Allocation Driver | Consolidation Treatment
- Component supply | Cost-plus method | Quantity, component unit price | Elimination
- Software/Cloud | Number of users/usage | MAU, API calls | Elimination
- Brand/Patents | Royalty rate | Sales/Product mix | Elimination
- Shared services | Cost center | Labor hours, sales | Allocation

As a rule, internal transactions are reviewed annually, and will take effect mid-period when there are major business reorganizations or market structure changes. Allocation rules are approved by the management committee, and their impact on KPIs is clearly shown in bridge materials. This framework is designed to ensure that each division’s accountable profit is assessed in line with business reality.

Governance, Audit, and Data Quality (Quarterly Closing Process)

To ensure the reliability of segment KPIs, including overseas profit, we conduct quarterly data quality checks and internal control assessments. For revenue recognition (IFRS 15), we verify the satisfaction of performance obligations and the determination of control transfer; for inventory valuation (IAS 2), we review lower of cost or net realizable value and provisions for slow-moving inventory. Depreciation of fixed assets (IAS 16/38) is reassessed to reflect actual utilization and technological obsolescence. Data is integrated from the ERP to the DWH, standardized and deduplicated via ETL, and then delivered to dashboards while preserving the audit trail.

- Quality checks: exchange rate translation differences, unresolved intercompany eliminations, anomaly detection (Z-score/rule-based).
- Access management: role-based authorization settings, edit request workflow for key KPIs.
- Audit support: sample matching, evidential traceability, journal reconciliation.
- Change management: impact analysis and backtesting when revising the account master or allocation keys.

Additionally, to accommodate delayed data from global sales subsidiaries and differences in holiday calendars, we have clarified post-closing roll-forward criteria and deadlines for correction requests. If a material KPI recalculation occurs, it is reported to the management committee and a restatement procedure is defined for the subsequent period. The entire process is reviewed by the audit firm and is subject to continuous improvement.

Medium-Term Strategy and Investment Policy (Priority Areas for FY2026–FY2028)

In the medium term, we aim for sustainable profit growth by enhancing added value and advancing service-based offerings in overseas markets. For home appliances, we will expand the lineup of high-efficiency, energy-saving models and promote subscription-based installation and maintenance. For IoT devices, we will focus on cloud integration and strengthened security, expanding data monetization models (devices plus services). For wearables, we will pursue healthcare integration and B2B use cases (workplace safety and on-site support) to raise both average selling prices and recurring revenue.

Investment allocation will be optimized as a portfolio from the perspectives of growth potential, capital efficiency, and risk. Key themes include reorganizing development sites, hiring software talent, automating manufacturing, enhancing quality laboratories, and strengthening overseas after-sales service networks. In ESG, we will focus on decarbonizing the supply chain, using recycled materials, improving repairability, and governing data privacy and AI ethics. We will adopt KPIs such as ROIC, free cash flow, subscription ARPU/churn, and SLO (service level objective) attainment rates, and assess progress in quarterly reviews.

We will also enhance profitability analysis by sales channel and consider the optimal mix of direct sales, e-commerce, mass retail, and B2B. We will use machine learning to support price elasticity estimation and attribution of promotion investments, and advance efforts to automatically optimize inventory strategies in line with regional demand fluctuations. Through these measures, we aim to increase the stability of overseas profits and mitigate the impact of sudden macroeconomic fluctuations.
